subject: hadith
description_en: The most authentic book after the Quran. A collection of hadith compiled over 16 years, selecting from 600,000 narrations.

subject: hadith
description_en: The second most authentic hadith collection. Known for its superior organization and methodology.

subject: hadith
description_en: Focuses on hadith relevant to Islamic jurisprudence (fiqh).

subject: hadith
description_en: Notable for grading each hadith and mentioning scholarly differences.

subject: tafsir
description_en: One of the most widely used and respected tafsirs. Relies heavily on hadith and narrations of the Salaf for explanation.

author_name_en: Jalal ad-Din al-Mahalli & Jalal ad-Din as-Suyuti
subject: tafsir
description_en: A concise and widely studied tafsir written by two scholars both named Jalal ad-Din.

subject: seerah
description_en: Award-winning biography of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ. Won first prize in a worldwide Seerah competition held by the Muslim World League in 1979.

subject: hadith
description_en: A compilation of hadith covering all aspects of a Muslim's daily life, organized thematically. One of the most widely read hadith compilations.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: A concise creed statement outlining the Athari aqeedah. One of the most studied texts in Islamic theology.

subject: hadith
description_en: A collection of 42 hadith covering the foundations of Islam, faith, and moral conduct. The most memorized hadith compilation worldwide.

subject: seerah
description_en: A comprehensive guide to the Prophet's life and practices, covering worship, daily life, medicine, warfare, and governance.

title_en: Al-Bidayah wan-Nihayah (The Beginning and the End)
subject: history
description_en: A comprehensive history from creation to the events of the 14th century AH. One of the most important works of Islamic historiography.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A modern fiqh manual presenting rulings based directly on Quran and Sunnah, comparing all four madhabs. Widely used as an accessible introduction to Islamic jurisprudence.

title_en: Ihya Ulum ad-Din (Revival of the Religious Sciences)
subject: general
description_en: A monumental work covering worship, daily life, vices, and virtues. Al-Ghazali's masterpiece integrating fiqh, theology, and spiritual purification.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: A foundational work on the objectives of Islamic law (maqasid ash-shariah). Revolutionized the field of usul al-fiqh.

subject: hadith
description_en: The most comprehensive and authoritative commentary on Sahih al-Bukhari. Considered the crown jewel of hadith commentary literature.

subject: hadith
description_en: The most famous and widely used commentary on Sahih Muslim. Covers fiqh rulings, hadith terminology, and scholarly opinions.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: A concise work on Islamic monotheism, using Quran and hadith to explain tawhid and its violations. Widely studied in Islamic education.

subject: fiqh
description_en: One of the most important and comprehensive works of comparative fiqh. Presents Hanbali positions while comparing with all major schools.

title_en: Siyar A'lam an-Nubala (Lives of Noble Figures)
subject: biography
description_en: A monumental biographical dictionary covering over 5,000 notable figures in Islamic history from the companions to adh-Dhahabi's era.

subject: hadith
description_en: One of the six canonical hadith collections (Kutub as-Sittah). Known for its strict grading criteria, an-Nasa'i's conditions for accepting narrators are considered the most rigorous among the six collections. The work focuses primarily on hadith related to legal rulings.

subject: hadith
description_en: The sixth of the canonical hadith collections. Contains approximately 4,341 hadith organized into chapters on legal rulings, virtues, and manners. While it includes some weak narrations not found in the other five collections, it remains an essential reference for Islamic jurisprudence.

subject: hadith
description_en: The earliest surviving collection of hadith and the first work to systematically compile hadith alongside the legal practice of Madinah. Imam Malik spent over 40 years refining this work, which became the foundation of the Maliki school of jurisprudence. It contains approximately 1,720 narrations.

subject: hadith
description_en: One of the largest hadith collections ever compiled, containing over 27,000 narrations organized by the companion who narrated them. Imam Ahmad selected these from over 750,000 hadith he had memorized. The Musnad is a primary source for hadith scholars and contains many narrations not found in the six canonical collections.

subject: hadith
description_en: An early hadith collection predating several of the six canonical works. Contains approximately 3,500 narrations organized by legal topic. Ad-Darimi was a teacher of both Imam al-Bukhari and Imam Muslim, and his collection is valued for its high-quality chains of narration and its unique introduction on the virtues of seeking knowledge.

subject: hadith
description_en: One of the earliest and most comprehensive hadith compilations, containing over 38,000 narrations including hadith of the Prophet, statements of the Companions, and rulings of the Tabi'in. Organized by legal topic, it is an indispensable source for understanding early Islamic legal reasoning and the opinions of the Salaf.

subject: hadith
description_en: Among the oldest surviving hadith compilations in Islam, compiled by the great Yemeni scholar Abdur-Razzaq ibn Hammam. Contains over 21,000 narrations, preserving the juristic opinions and hadith transmission traditions of the early Yemeni and Hijazi schools. An essential primary source for studying the formative period of Islamic law.

subject: hadith
description_en: A dedicated collection by the author of Sahih al-Bukhari focusing exclusively on hadith related to manners, etiquette, and moral conduct. Contains 1,322 narrations covering topics from greeting people and visiting the sick to maintaining family ties and showing kindness to animals. Widely used as a manual of Islamic character development.

subject: hadith
description_en: A concise collection of hadith that form the legal basis for Shafi'i fiqh rulings. Ibn Hajar carefully selected approximately 1,596 hadith from the major collections, noting their grades and the opinions of hadith scholars regarding their authenticity. One of the most widely taught texts in Islamic seminaries worldwide.

subject: hadith
description_en: An expanded revision of al-Baghawi's Masabih as-Sunnah, adding source attributions, grading, and over 1,500 additional hadith. Organized thematically into 29 books covering all aspects of faith and practice. It became one of the most popular hadith compilations in the Indian subcontinent and Central Asia, with multiple commentaries written on it.

subject: hadith
description_en: A concise collection of approximately 420 hadith agreed upon by al-Bukhari and Muslim (muttafaq alayh), selected specifically for their relevance to legal rulings. Because every hadith in this collection meets the highest standard of authenticity, it is widely used as an introductory text for students of fiqh al-hadith. Numerous commentaries have been written on it.

subject: hadith
description_en: A hadith collection organized in a unique thematic arrangement rather than the traditional chapter-based format. Ibn Hibban applied strict criteria for authentication and included only narrations he considered sahih. Contains approximately 7,500 hadith. The work is highly valued by hadith scholars for its methodology and for preserving many narrations with unique chains of transmission.

subject: hadith
description_en: A collection of hadith that al-Hakim argued meet the criteria of al-Bukhari and Muslim but were not included in their Sahih collections. Contains approximately 8,800 narrations. While scholars like adh-Dhahabi noted that al-Hakim was sometimes lenient in his grading, the work remains an important supplementary source to the two Sahih collections.

subject: hadith
description_en: A modern hadith authentication project by the 20th century's foremost hadith verifier. Al-Albani collected and authenticated hadith from across classical sources, providing detailed analysis of each chain of narration. The series, along with its companion Silsilat ad-Da'ifah (weak narrations), represents the most extensive modern effort to grade hadith outside the classical canon.

title_ar: جامع البيان عن تأويل آي القرآن
subject: tafsir
description_en: The earliest complete tafsir that has survived intact and the foundation of all subsequent Quran commentary. At-Tabari compiled interpretations from the Prophet, the Companions, and the Tabi'in with full chains of narration, then analyzed the linguistic and grammatical aspects of each verse. Virtually every later mufassir relied on this work as a primary source.

subject: tafsir
description_en: A comprehensive tafsir with a strong emphasis on extracting legal rulings (ahkam) from the Quran. Al-Qurtubi, a Maliki scholar, presents opinions from all four schools of fiqh, making this one of the most balanced and encyclopedic works of legal tafsir. It covers grammar, variant readings, occasions of revelation, and abrogation alongside detailed fiqh discussions.

subject: tafsir
description_en: Known as 'The Great Tafsir' (at-Tafsir al-Kabir), this is the most prominent tafsir from the Ash'ari theological tradition. Ar-Razi brings extensive philosophical, rational, and scientific analysis to Quranic interpretation, engaging with the arguments of Mu'tazili and other theological schools. The work is distinguished by its systematic approach to refuting objections and its integration of the natural sciences of its era.

subject: tafsir
description_en: A moderately sized tafsir that strikes a balance between the narration-based approach of at-Tabari and the more concise style of later works. Al-Baghawi, known as 'Muhyi as-Sunnah' (Reviver of the Sunnah), focused on sound narrations and clear explanations while avoiding fabricated reports. Ibn Taymiyyah praised it as being better than Tafsir al-Tha'labi because al-Baghawi avoided weak and fabricated narrations.

title_ar: تيسير الكريم الرحمن في تفسير كلام المنان
subject: tafsir
description_en: A modern, accessible tafsir written in clear, simple Arabic without complex technical terminology. As-Sa'di focused on extracting practical benefits and spiritual lessons from each verse, making this work popular among general readers and students. Despite its conciseness, it is considered thorough in covering the meanings of the Quran from a Salafi theological perspective.

subject: tafsir
description_en: A medium-length tafsir that excels in presenting multiple scholarly opinions concisely. Ibn al-Jawzi, the great Hanbali polymath, compiled the major interpretive positions for each verse and organized them clearly, making this work an invaluable reference for comparative tafsir study. His approach emphasizes clarity and accessibility without sacrificing scholarly depth.

title_ar: أضواء البيان في إيضاح القرآن بالقرآن
subject: tafsir
description_en: A distinguished modern tafsir that interprets the Quran primarily through the Quran itself (tafsir al-Quran bil-Quran). Ash-Shinqiti, a Mauritanian scholar of immense erudition, combined Quranic cross-referencing with detailed discussions of fiqh, usul, Arabic grammar, and hadith. The work is renowned for its depth of legal analysis and its methodological rigor.

subject: tafsir
description_en: A massive compilation of narration-based tafsir (tafsir bil-ma'thur). As-Suyuti gathered interpretive narrations from the Prophet, the Companions, and the Tabi'in from dozens of earlier sources, many of which are now lost. While it does not include chains of narration (as-Suyuti's abridgment), it preserves a vast treasury of early exegetical material and is an essential reference for hadith-based Quran interpretation.

subject: tafsir
description_en: One of the most influential early tafsirs that bridges the narration-based and opinion-based approaches. Ath-Tha'labi compiled interpretations with full chains of narration and incorporated extensive linguistic analysis, stories of the prophets, and literary appreciation of the Quran. Many later mufassirin, including al-Baghawi and al-Qurtubi, drew heavily from this work.

subject: tafsir
description_en: A specialized tafsir focused exclusively on the legal verses of the Quran from the Maliki perspective. Abu Bakr ibn al-Arabi, the prominent Andalusian judge and scholar (not to be confused with the Sufi Ibn Arabi), provides detailed comparative fiqh discussions for each verse containing a legal ruling. The work is considered one of the three greatest works on ahkam al-Quran alongside those of al-Jassas (Hanafi) and al-Kiya al-Harrasi (Shafi'i).

subject: fiqh
description_en: The foundational text of the Shafi'i school of jurisprudence, compiled from Imam ash-Shafi'i's dictations in Egypt during his final years. It represents his mature legal methodology (the 'new' school), covering virtually every area of fiqh with evidence from Quran, Sunnah, ijma, and qiyas. The work also contains ash-Shafi'i's debates with scholars of other schools, making it a valuable source for comparative fiqh.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The primary reference work for the Maliki school of jurisprudence. Sahnun compiled it from the legal responses of Ibn al-Qasim, Imam Malik's most prominent student, covering the entire spectrum of Islamic legal rulings. The work preserves Imam Malik's positions as transmitted through his student and became the most authoritative reference for Maliki jurists in North Africa and beyond.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The most studied and authoritative fiqh manual in the Hanafi school. Al-Marghinani's work became the standard reference for Hanafi jurisprudence across the Ottoman Empire, Central Asia, and the Indian subcontinent. It presents legal rulings with evidence and reasoning, noting differences of opinion within the Hanafi school. Over 70 commentaries and super-commentaries have been written on it.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A unique work of comparative fiqh by the famous Andalusian philosopher and jurist. Rather than advocating one school, Ibn Rushd systematically presents the points of agreement and disagreement among the four madhabs, analyzes the root causes of their differences (textual, methodological, or linguistic), and traces each position to its evidence. An indispensable text for understanding why scholars differ.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A highly condensed abridgment of Ibn Qudamah's al-Muqni, presenting the relied-upon positions of the Hanbali school in the most concise language possible. Despite its brevity, it covers all major areas of fiqh. It became the most widely memorized and studied Hanbali fiqh text, particularly in the Arabian Peninsula, with dozens of commentaries written on it, the most famous being ash-Sharh al-Mumti by Ibn Uthaymin.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A comprehensive yet accessible Hanbali fiqh manual by the author of al-Mughni. While al-Mughni is encyclopedic and comparative, al-Kafi focuses on presenting the Hanbali school's positions clearly with their evidence from Quran and Sunnah. It serves as a middle-level text between the brief al-Umdah and the exhaustive al-Mughni, making it ideal for intermediate students of Hanbali jurisprudence.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The most authoritative fiqh manual of the later Shafi'i school. An-Nawawi abridged and refined al-Rafi'i's al-Muharrar, selecting the strongest positions and correcting errors. This concise text became the standard reference for Shafi'i jurisprudence, particularly in Southeast Asia, Egypt, and Yemen. Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's Tuhfat al-Muhtaj and ar-Ramli's Nihayat al-Muhtaj are its two most important commentaries.

subject: fiqh
description_en: An-Nawawi's monumental commentary on ash-Shirazi's al-Muhadhdhab, widely considered the greatest work of comparative fiqh in the Shafi'i tradition. An-Nawawi completed only a portion before his death, but the existing volumes are encyclopedic in scope, discussing the evidence from Quran, Sunnah, and scholarly consensus for each ruling and presenting opinions from all four schools. Later scholars continued the work.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A well-organized Hanafi fiqh manual that explains the reasoning behind each ruling in the relied-upon Hanafi text al-Mukhtar. Al-Mawsili provides the evidences from Quran and Sunnah, the views of the three major Hanafi imams (Abu Hanifah, Abu Yusuf, and Muhammad ash-Shaybani), and the rational justifications for each position. Widely used in Islamic universities as a teaching text for Hanafi fiqh.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The definitive reference for fatwa in the Hanafi school, serving as a super-commentary on al-Haskafi's ad-Durr al-Mukhtar. Ibn Abidin synthesized centuries of Hanafi legal scholarship and provided definitive resolutions to disputed questions within the school. This work is the primary reference consulted by Hanafi muftis worldwide for issuing legal opinions.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A concise beginner-level Shafi'i fiqh manual widely studied in Southeast Asia, East Africa, and the Hadrami diaspora communities. It covers the essentials of worship (purification, prayer, zakat, fasting, and Hajj) in clear, accessible language. The text became a cornerstone of Islamic education in the Malay Archipelago, with multiple translations and commentaries in Malay, Javanese, and Swahili.

subject: fiqh
description_en: One of the most important commentaries on Khalil's Mukhtasar, the most condensed and authoritative summary of Maliki fiqh. Al-Kharshi's commentary unpacks Khalil's extremely compressed text, explaining its terminology, providing evidences, and noting the different views within the Maliki school. It remains a standard reference in Maliki institutions across West and North Africa.

title_ar: الإنصاف في معرفة الراجح من الخلاف
title_en: Al-Insaf fi Ma'rifat ar-Rajih min al-Khilaf
subject: fiqh
description_en: The definitive work for determining the relied-upon position (mu'tamad) within the Hanbali school when internal disagreements exist. Al-Mardawi meticulously examined all conflicting opinions transmitted from Imam Ahmad and later Hanbali scholars, then identified the strongest position for each issue. Any serious student or mufti of the Hanbali school considers this work indispensable.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: A masterwork on the philosophy and methodology of Islamic legal reasoning. Ibn al-Qayyim discusses the qualifications of a mufti, the relationship between text and reason, the wisdom behind Islamic rulings, and the principles of juristic analogy and legal stratagems. The work is both a manual of usul al-fiqh and a passionate defense of deriving rulings from evidence rather than blind imitation (taqlid).

subject: fiqh
description_en: A foundational work on Islamic legal maxims (qawa'id fiqhiyyah) from the Shafi'i perspective. As-Suyuti organized the universal principles that underpin Islamic jurisprudence, such as 'certainty is not removed by doubt' and 'harm must be eliminated.' These maxims serve as tools for deriving rulings in new situations and have been incorporated into modern Islamic legal codes, including the Ottoman Majallah.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: The most widely accepted creedal statement in Sunni Islam. At-Tahawi, a Hanafi jurist, summarized the beliefs of Ahl us-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah in a concise text that scholars of all four schools have endorsed. It covers the attributes of Allah, prophethood, the unseen, predestination, and the status of the Companions. Dozens of commentaries have been written on it, the most famous by Ibn Abi al-Izz.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: The most celebrated commentary on the Tahawiyyah creed. Ibn Abi al-Izz expanded each creedal statement with extensive evidence from Quran, Sunnah, and the statements of the Salaf, while refuting deviant theological positions. The commentary is heavily influenced by the methodology of Ibn Taymiyyah and Ibn al-Qayyim, and is one of the most widely taught aqeedah texts in Islamic universities today.

title_ar: لمعة الاعتقاد الهادي إلى سبيل الرشاد
subject: aqeedah
description_en: A brief creedal text presenting the Athari aqeedah of the Hanbali school. Ibn Qudamah compiled the essential beliefs of Ahl us-Sunnah with evidence from Quran and Sunnah, covering divine attributes, the Quran's nature, predestination, and the status of the Companions. Its conciseness makes it one of the first texts memorized by students of aqeedah in the Hanbali tradition.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: A creedal work by the founder of the Ash'ari school, written after his famous departure from Mu'tazili theology. In this text, al-Ash'ari explicitly aligns himself with the creed of Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal and the Ahl al-Hadith, affirming divine attributes without ta'wil (figurative interpretation). The work is significant for understanding the development of Ash'ari theology and its original proximity to the Athari position.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: A detailed fatwa written in response to a question from the people of Hama, Syria, about the divine attributes mentioned in the Quran and Sunnah. Ibn Taymiyyah presents the position of the Salaf on affirming Allah's attributes without likening them to creation (tashbih) or negating them (ta'til), citing extensive evidence from the early scholars. The work is a major reference in Athari theology.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: Ibn Taymiyyah's magnum opus on the relationship between reason and revelation. In this massive work, he systematically refutes the claim that rational proofs can override scriptural evidence, engaging with the arguments of philosophers, Mu'tazilis, Ash'aris, and others. The work is considered one of the most sophisticated philosophical-theological treatises in Islamic history.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: A detailed response to the Shia scholar Ibn al-Mutahhar al-Hilli's Minhaj al-Karamah. Ibn Taymiyyah refutes Shia theological claims point by point, while also addressing issues of creed, politics, and history from the Sunni perspective. The work is both a polemic and a comprehensive exposition of Sunni positions on the Companions, the caliphate, and theological principles.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: An early hadith-based work on the divine attributes compiled by the great hadith master Ibn Khuzaymah, author of the famous Sahih Ibn Khuzaymah. The work gathers narrations affirming Allah's attributes from the Prophet and the Companions, organized by topic. It represents the methodology of the early Ahl al-Hadith scholars in establishing creed through authentic narrations rather than philosophical reasoning.

title_ar: شرح أصول اعتقاد أهل السنة والجماعة
subject: aqeedah
description_en: A massive compilation of creedal statements and narrations from the Prophet, the Companions, the Tabi'in, and later scholars of Ahl us-Sunnah, organized by theological topic. Al-Lalika'i gathered these reports to demonstrate the consensus of the early Muslim community on core beliefs. The work is an invaluable primary source for understanding the creedal positions of the Salaf on every major theological question.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: A modern compendium of Athari creed that gathers the positions of the Salaf on every major theological point with extensive citations from classical sources. The author presents the creed of Ahl us-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah as understood by the early generations, providing a systematic reference that draws from dozens of classical creedal texts. Useful as both a study text and a reference for scholars and students.

subject: seerah
description_en: The most famous and widely relied upon biography of the Prophet Muhammad. Ibn Hisham edited and annotated the earlier Sirah of Ibn Ishaq, removing unreliable material and adding linguistic and historical notes. This work is the primary source for virtually every later seerah writer and preserves the earliest connected narrative of the Prophet's life from birth to death.

subject: biography
description_en: The earliest biographical dictionary of the Prophet, his Companions, and the Tabi'in arranged in generational layers (tabaqat). Ibn Sa'd, a student of al-Waqidi, compiled detailed biographies of thousands of early Muslims, preserving information about their lineage, conversion, participation in battles, narration of hadith, and personal qualities. An indispensable source for the study of early Islamic history.

title_en: Tarikh at-Tabari (History of Prophets and Kings)
subject: history
description_en: The most comprehensive work of Islamic historiography, covering world history from creation through the prophets, the life of Muhammad, the Rashidun caliphate, the Umayyads, and the Abbasids up to 915 CE. At-Tabari preserved multiple accounts of each event with full chains of narration, allowing readers to evaluate conflicting reports. The work was translated into a 39-volume English edition by SUNY Press.

subject: history
description_en: A comprehensive world history continuing where at-Tabari left off, covering events up to 1231 CE including the devastating Mongol invasions. Ibn al-Athir synthesized at-Tabari's multiple-narration approach into a single coherent narrative, making the history more accessible. The work is the primary source for the history of the Crusades from the Muslim perspective and the Mongol destruction of the Abbasid Caliphate.

subject: seerah
description_en: A comprehensive compilation of narrations about the lives, sacrifices, and virtues of the Companions of the Prophet. Al-Kandhlawi drew from dozens of classical sources to paint a vivid picture of the first Muslim generation, organized thematically by their qualities: faith, sacrifice, migration, patience, generosity, and worship. The work is widely read in the Tablighi Jama'at movement and in Islamic schools worldwide.

subject: biography
description_en: The most comprehensive biographical dictionary of the Companions of the Prophet, covering over 12,000 entries. Ibn Hajar verified each person's status as a Companion using strict hadith methodology, distinguishing between confirmed Companions, those whose companionship is disputed, and those mistakenly listed in earlier works. It is the definitive reference for identifying and authenticating the Companions.

subject: history
description_en: A groundbreaking introduction to Ibn Khaldun's universal history, widely regarded as the first work of sociology, historiography, and philosophy of history. Ibn Khaldun analyzes the rise and fall of civilizations through his concept of asabiyyah (social cohesion), examining economics, education, urbanization, and political power. The work transcended Islamic scholarship and influenced modern Western social sciences.

subject: seerah
description_en: A beloved work on the rights, qualities, and miracles of the Prophet Muhammad. Qadi Iyad, the great Maliki scholar of al-Andalus, compiled everything related to the Prophet's sublime character, physical description, miracles, and the obligation to honor him. The work became so popular that scholars said 'no house should be without ash-Shifa,' and it has been continuously studied across the Muslim world for nearly a millennium.

subject: seerah
description_en: A focused seerah work by the author of al-Bidayah wan-Nihayah, extracted and reorganized from the seerah sections of his larger historical work. Ibn Kathir applied his hadith expertise to evaluate the chains of narration for each event in the Prophet's life, making this one of the most critically rigorous classical biographies of the Prophet. It covers from pre-Islamic Arabia through the Prophet's death.

subject: biography
description_en: A masterpiece of biographical literature containing detailed entries on 865 notable figures from across the Islamic world. Ibn Khallikan, a Kurdish scholar and judge, combined scholarly precision with elegant prose, providing birth and death dates, lineage, scholarly achievements, and memorable anecdotes. The work is considered the finest example of the Islamic biographical dictionary genre and remains essential for identifying historical figures.

title_ar: مدارج السالكين بين منازل إياك نعبد وإياك نستعين
subject: ethics
description_en: A profound work on the spiritual journey to Allah, structured as a commentary on al-Harawi's Manazil as-Sa'irin. Ibn al-Qayyim discusses the stations of the spiritual path (repentance, patience, gratitude, fear, hope, love, reliance, etc.) while grounding every concept in Quran and Sunnah. He corrects Sufi excesses while affirming the reality of spiritual purification, making this the most balanced work on Islamic spirituality from the Athari tradition.

subject: ethics
description_en: A work on the virtues of dhikr (remembrance of Allah) and its transformative effects on the heart and soul. Ibn al-Qayyim enumerates over seventy benefits of consistent dhikr, discusses the types of remembrance prescribed in the Sunnah, and describes how dhikr protects against spiritual diseases. The book is both a practical guide and a motivational text that has inspired Muslims for centuries.

subject: ethics
description_en: An abridgment of Ibn al-Jawzi's Minhaj al-Qasidin, which itself was a purified version of al-Ghazali's Ihya Ulum ad-Din. Ibn Qudamah removed the weak and fabricated hadith from al-Ghazali's work while preserving its spiritual and ethical content. The result is a concise, hadith-verified guide to Islamic ethics and self-purification that provides the benefits of the Ihya without its problematic narrations.

subject: ethics
description_en: An extensive commentary on an-Nawawi's Forty Hadith plus eight additional hadith, totaling fifty narrations. Ibn Rajab expands each hadith with a comprehensive discussion of its chains, related narrations, fiqh rulings, and spiritual lessons. The depth of scholarship far exceeds a typical hadith commentary, making this work simultaneously a reference in hadith sciences, fiqh, and spiritual development.

title_ar: الجواب الكافي لمن سأل عن الدواء الشافي
subject: ethics
description_en: Written in response to a question about the cure for sins and their spiritual consequences. Ibn al-Qayyim provides a penetrating analysis of how sins affect the heart, body, provision, and relationships, and prescribes a detailed spiritual remedy rooted in Quran and Sunnah. The work is both a diagnosis of spiritual ailments and a prescription for recovery, written with the passion and insight characteristic of Ibn al-Qayyim.

subject: ethics
description_en: A collection of spiritual insights, reflections, and benefits gathered by Ibn al-Qayyim from his study of the Quran, Sunnah, and observations of life. The work covers topics from the nature of the heart and the reality of worship to the wisdom behind divine decrees and the virtues of patience. Each entry is a self-contained gem of spiritual wisdom, making this a widely read devotional text.

author_name_en: Ibn Rajab al-Hanbali, Ibn al-Qayyim & al-Ghazali
subject: ethics
description_en: A compiled work drawing from the writings of three major scholars on the purification of the soul. It covers the diseases of the heart (envy, arrogance, showing off, anger, attachment to the world), their causes, symptoms, and cures as prescribed by the Quran and Sunnah. The compilation provides a practical approach to tazkiyah grounded in orthodox Islamic scholarship.

subject: ethics
description_en: One of the earliest works on Islamic ethics and self-accounting (muhasabah). Al-Muhasibi, whose name derives from his emphasis on self-examination, outlines the inner dimensions of sincerity, the dangers of the ego, the importance of consistent self-reflection, and the path to genuine devotion. The work influenced all later writers on Islamic spirituality, including al-Ghazali, who drew extensively from al-Muhasibi's methodology.

subject: ethics
description_en: A comprehensive manual of Islamic etiquette and character covering every aspect of a Muslim's social and personal life. Ibn Muflih, a student of Ibn Taymiyyah, compiled rulings and recommendations on speech, eating, travel, visiting, greeting, neighborly relations, and dozens of other topics. The work uniquely combines fiqh rulings with ethical guidance, making it both a legal and a spiritual reference.

title_en: Ad-Da' wad-Dawa' (The Disease and the Cure)
subject: ethics
description_en: Also known as al-Jawab al-Kafi in some editions, this work addresses the devastating effects of sins on the individual and society, and prescribes the remedy of sincere repentance and consistent worship. Ibn al-Qayyim draws from the Quran, prophetic traditions, and the wisdom of the Salaf to create a deeply moving account of the soul's struggle and its path to healing through obedience to Allah.

subject: arabic
description_en: The most widely memorized introductory text on Arabic grammar in the Islamic world. Ibn Ajurrum condensed the fundamentals of Arabic syntax (nahw) into a few pages covering the parts of speech, declension, and basic sentence structure. For over seven centuries, this tiny text has served as the first grammar book for millions of students across Africa, the Middle East, and Southeast Asia. Over 100 commentaries have been written on it.

subject: arabic
description_en: A thousand-line poem (alfiyyah) that comprehensively covers Arabic grammar and morphology. Ibn Malik, the Andalusian grammarian, condensed the entire science of Arabic nahw and sarf into elegant, memorizable verse. The poem superseded all earlier grammar versifications and remains the standard advanced grammar text in traditional Islamic education. Ibn Aqil's commentary is the most widely studied explanation of this work.

subject: arabic
description_en: The largest and most comprehensive classical Arabic dictionary, containing over 80,000 entries. Ibn Manzur compiled this monumental work from five earlier dictionaries, organizing entries by root letters and providing Quranic verses, hadith, poetry, and prose examples for each word. It remains the primary reference for classical Arabic lexicography and is indispensable for scholars of tafsir, hadith, and Arabic literature.

subject: arabic
description_en: An intermediate Arabic grammar text that bridges the gap between the elementary al-Ajrumiyyah and the advanced Alfiyyah of Ibn Malik. Ibn Hisham, widely regarded as the greatest grammarian after Sibawayh, wrote this text with its own commentary (sharh) for students who have mastered the basics and need a systematic expansion. The clear organization and ample examples make it an enduringly popular grammar textbook.

subject: arabic
description_en: The most widely used textbook on Arabic rhetoric (balaghah) in modern Islamic education. The authors systematically present the three branches of balaghah: ma'ani (meanings), bayan (clarity), and badi' (embellishment) with clear definitions, Quranic examples, and graded exercises. The work made the traditionally difficult science of Arabic rhetoric accessible to students and remains a standard text in universities and seminaries worldwide.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: The first systematic treatise on Islamic legal theory (usul al-fiqh) ever written. Imam ash-Shafi'i laid out the foundations of how to derive legal rulings from the Quran, Sunnah, ijma (consensus), and qiyas (analogical reasoning). He established the hierarchy of legal sources and the principles of abrogation, general versus specific texts, and the authority of the Sunnah. Every later work of usul al-fiqh builds upon the framework established in this groundbreaking text.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: A concise primer on the principles of Islamic jurisprudence that covers the essential topics in just a few pages: types of legal rulings, linguistic principles, commands and prohibitions, general and specific texts, abrogation, consensus, analogical reasoning, and ijtihad. Despite its brevity, it became one of the most widely taught introductory texts on usul al-fiqh, with numerous commentaries written on it across the centuries.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: The most important work of usul al-fiqh in the Hanbali school. Ibn Qudamah based it on al-Ghazali's al-Mustasfa while incorporating the Hanbali perspective on legal theory, particularly regarding the authority of Companion opinions and the scope of istishab (presumption of continuity). The work is studied alongside al-Muwafaqat as one of the two pillars of Sunni legal theory.

title_ar: إرشاد الفحول إلى تحقيق الحق من علم الأصول
subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: A comprehensive work on usul al-fiqh by the great Yemeni mujtahid ash-Shawkani. The author surveys all major positions in legal theory across the schools, evaluates them critically, and argues for the strongest view based on evidence. Ash-Shawkani advocates for ijtihad over taqlid throughout the work, making it both a textbook of legal theory and a manifesto for evidence-based jurisprudence. It remains widely studied in Islamic universities.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: One of the four foundational works of usul al-fiqh in Sunni Islam, alongside ar-Risalah, al-Mu'tamad, and al-Burhan. Al-Ghazali systematized legal theory with philosophical precision, incorporating Aristotelian logic into the methodology of deriving Islamic rulings. His treatment of maslahah (public interest) and the five essential objectives of the Shariah (preservation of religion, life, intellect, lineage, and property) profoundly influenced all later scholarship on the maqasid ash-shariah.

subject: comparative_religion
description_en: A pioneering work of comparative religion and heresiography. Ash-Shahrastani systematically catalogs and describes the beliefs of Islamic sects, other Abrahamic religions, ancient Greek philosophies, and Indian religions. His methodology of presenting each group's beliefs from their own perspective, rather than merely refuting them, was remarkably objective for its time and makes this work a foundational text in the study of religions.

subject: comparative_religion
description_en: The most detailed work of comparative religion in classical Islamic literature. Ibn Hazm, the great Zahiri scholar of Andalusia, critically examines Judaism, Christianity, Zoroastrianism, and the various Islamic sects with his characteristic sharp logic. His textual criticism of the Bible represents one of the earliest systematic attempts at biblical criticism, centuries before similar efforts in European scholarship.

subject: fiqh
description_en: Written at the request of Caliph Harun ar-Rashid, this is the earliest surviving work on Islamic public finance and taxation. Abu Yusuf, the chief student of Abu Hanifah and the first Chief Justice of the Abbasid Caliphate, covers land tax, poll tax, trade regulations, public treasury, and the economic responsibilities of the state. It remains a primary source for Islamic economics and governance.

subject: history
description_en: A groundbreaking work of social science that preceded modern sociology, historiography, and economics by centuries. Ibn Khaldun analyzes the rise and fall of civilizations through his concept of asabiyyah (social cohesion), examines economic cycles, population dynamics, and the relationship between nomadic and urban societies. Widely regarded as one of the most important intellectual works in human history.

subject: hadith_sciences
description_en: The most comprehensive biographical dictionary of hadith narrators in Islamic scholarship. Al-Mizzi compiled detailed entries for every narrator mentioned in the six canonical hadith collections and their supplements, covering their teachers, students, and the scholarly evaluation of their reliability. This work is the essential reference for hadith authentication.

subject: hadith_sciences
description_en: The most widely studied introductory text on hadith terminology (mustalah al-hadith). Ibn Hajar condensed the entire science of evaluating hadith narrations into a concise, logical framework that covers the types of hadith chains, narrator evaluation, conditions for authenticity, and types of weakness. It is the standard first text studied by students of hadith sciences worldwide.

subject: hadith_sciences
description_en: A commentary on an-Nawawi's Taqrib, this work is the most detailed and widely referenced intermediate-level text on hadith methodology. As-Suyuti explains every term and concept in hadith sciences with abundant examples from classical scholarship. The work covers chain analysis, narrator criticism, types of transmission, and the rules for accepting and rejecting narrations.

subject: quran_sciences
description_en: The most comprehensive work on the sciences of the Quran (ulum al-Quran). As-Suyuti covers 80 distinct sciences related to the Quran including its compilation, variant readings, occasions of revelation, abrogation, Meccan and Medinan verses, linguistic miracles, and legal derivation. This encyclopedic work remains the standard reference for Quranic studies.

subject: quran_sciences
description_en: A pioneering systematic treatment of the sciences of the Quran that preceded and influenced as-Suyuti's al-Itqan. Az-Zarkashi organized the Quranic sciences into 47 types, covering textual criticism, linguistic analysis, legal derivation, and the philosophy of Quranic interpretation. The work's rigorous methodology established it as a foundational text in the field.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The largest and most comprehensive work of Hanafi fiqh ever compiled. As-Sarakhsi dictated this 30-volume encyclopedia from memory while imprisoned. It covers every branch of Islamic law in exhaustive detail, presenting the positions of Abu Hanifah and his students with their evidences and reasoning. The work is the ultimate reference for Hanafi jurisprudence.

subject: hadith
description_en: The standard commentary on Sahih Muslim, an-Nawawi explains each hadith with its linguistic meanings, fiqh rulings, and the positions of the four schools. He also provides valuable discussions on hadith methodology, narrator biographies, and points of scholarly consensus and disagreement. This work complemented his Minhaj at-Talibin in fiqh to form the core of Shafi'i scholarship.

subject: biography
description_en: The most comprehensive biographical dictionary of the Prophet's Companions, containing over 12,000 entries. Ibn Hajar compiled and critically evaluated all available information about each Companion, correcting errors in earlier works and establishing definitive identifications. The work is the standard reference for verifying whether a person was actually a Companion of the Prophet.

title_en: Miftah Dar as-Sa'adah (Key to the Abode of Happiness)
subject: general
description_en: A wide-ranging work that begins as a treatise on the virtue of knowledge and expands into a comprehensive discussion of divine wisdom in creation. Ibn al-Qayyim demonstrates Allah's wisdom in the design of the human body, animal kingdom, plants, celestial bodies, and the spiritual world. The work is simultaneously a celebration of Islamic intellectualism and a devotional meditation on the signs of the Creator.

subject: ethics
description_en: A detailed exposition of Satan's traps and how to escape them. Ibn al-Qayyim identifies the various methods by which Shaytan misleads human beings and prescribes specific remedies from the Quran and Sunnah for each one. The work covers the diseases of the heart, the importance of sincerity, the reality of repentance, and the techniques of spiritual warfare that every Muslim needs.

subject: ethics
description_en: A penetrating analysis of how Shaytan deceives people from every walk of life. Ibn al-Jawzi examines how scholars, worshippers, ascetics, Sufis, merchants, rulers, and common people are each susceptible to different forms of self-deception and Satanic suggestion. The work is both a warning against spiritual complacency and a guide to authentic, balanced religious practice.

subject: biography
description_en: A biographical dictionary of the most pious and virtuous individuals in Islamic history, from the prophets through the early generations. Ibn al-Jawzi selected the most inspiring stories of worship, sacrifice, and devotion to present models of spiritual excellence. The work covers prophets, companions, tabi'in, and later scholars, organized chronologically and geographically.

subject: seerah
description_en: One of the most beloved works on the Prophet Muhammad in Islamic literature. Qadi Iyad, the great Maliki scholar of Ceuta, compiled everything related to the Prophet's unique status: his miracles, his physical and moral qualities, the obligation to love and honor him, and the legal rulings regarding those who disparage him. This work has been continuously studied and venerated across the Muslim world for nearly a millennium.

subject: seerah
description_en: A hadith collection dedicated to describing the Prophet's physical appearance, character, habits, and daily life. At-Tirmidhi compiled 415 narrations covering how the Prophet ate, drank, dressed, slept, laughed, cried, walked, and interacted with people. The work paints an intimate, detailed portrait of the Prophet's personality and manner that brings him to life for readers across the centuries.

subject: seerah
description_en: The award-winning modern seerah that won first prize in the Muslim World League's worldwide competition in 1979. Mubarakpuri presents a thoroughly researched, chronologically organized, and accessible account of the Prophet's life that draws from classical sources while presenting the material in a modern narrative style. It has been translated into dozens of languages and is the most widely read seerah in the contemporary Muslim world.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A concise manual of Shafi'i fiqh by al-Ghazali that summarizes the relied-upon positions of the school. This work served as the basis for al-Rafi'i's al-Aziz and ultimately led to an-Nawawi's Minhaj at-Talibin, forming the chain of texts that defines Shafi'i jurisprudence. Al-Ghazali's clear prose and systematic organization made complex legal discussions accessible to students.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A compilation of the fundamental legal maxims upon which Islamic jurisprudence is built. The five universal maxims are: actions are judged by intentions; certainty is not overruled by doubt; hardship brings ease; harm must be removed; and custom is given legal consideration. These maxims, distilled from thousands of individual rulings, allow scholars to derive new rulings for unprecedented situations while maintaining consistency with the spirit of the Shariah.

subject: philosophy
description_en: A landmark work in the history of philosophy where al-Ghazali systematically critiques 20 propositions of Aristotelian-Neoplatonic philosophy as adopted by Muslim philosophers like Ibn Sina and al-Farabi. He argues that three of their positions constitute outright disbelief: the eternity of the world, God's ignorance of particulars, and the denial of bodily resurrection. This work reshaped the relationship between philosophy and theology in Islam.

subject: philosophy
description_en: Al-Ghazali's spiritual autobiography recounting his intellectual crisis and the journey that led him from the heights of academic fame through radical skepticism to eventual spiritual certainty through Sufism. Often compared to Augustine's Confessions, this work describes al-Ghazali's engagement with theology, philosophy, Ismailism, and Sufism, and his conclusion that experiential knowledge of God transcends rational argumentation.

title_en: Hujjat Allah al-Balighah (The Conclusive Argument from God)
subject: general
description_en: A groundbreaking work that attempts to explain the wisdom and rationale behind Islamic law and practice. Shah Waliullah, the great 18th-century Indian scholar, sought to demonstrate that every ruling in Islam serves a rational purpose connected to human well-being. The work synthesizes hadith scholarship, fiqh, philosophy, and tasawwuf into a unified framework, making it one of the most intellectually ambitious works in later Islamic scholarship.

title_ar: معارج القدس في مدارج معرفة النفس
subject: ethics
description_en: A work on self-knowledge and the stages of the soul's journey toward God. Al-Ghazali describes the faculties of the human soul, the hierarchy of knowledge, and the progressive unveiling of divine realities to the sincere seeker. The work bridges philosophy and spirituality, presenting a systematic account of how the soul can ascend from mere sensory awareness to direct experiential knowledge of the divine.

subject: general
description_en: A massive compilation of Ibn Taymiyyah's legal opinions, theological treatises, and responses to questions on virtually every topic in Islam. Collected and organized by scholars after his death, this 37-volume work covers creed, worship, jurisprudence, Sufism, logic, philosophy, and polemics. It is the single most comprehensive source for understanding Ibn Taymiyyah's thought and methodology.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: A treatise on the obligation to follow the straight path and avoid imitating non-Muslims in their religious practices and customs. Ibn Taymiyyah discusses the principles of Islamic distinctiveness (mukhalafah), the evidence from the Quran and Sunnah, and the scholarly consensus on maintaining the unique identity of the Muslim community. The work addresses festivals, customs, dress, and other areas where external influence had crept into Muslim practice.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: A concise treatise addressing the common arguments used to justify acts that contradict pure monotheism (tawhid). Muhammad ibn Abdul Wahhab responds to twelve specific doubts or objections that people raised against his call to pure tawhid, using Quranic verses, hadith, and the statements of classical scholars as evidence. The work is a companion text to his Kitab at-Tawhid.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: A short but foundational text on the three questions every person will be asked in the grave: Who is your Lord? What is your religion? Who is your Prophet? Muhammad ibn Abdul Wahhab builds from these three questions into a concise explanation of tawhid, Islam, and the prophetic mission. Despite its brevity, this text is one of the most widely taught creed primers in the Muslim world.

subject: quran_sciences
description_en: A guide to the etiquette of learning, teaching, and reciting the Quran. An-Nawawi covers the virtues of Quran recitation, the manners of the student and teacher, rules of tajwid, the etiquette of handling the mushaf, the merits of specific surahs, and practical advice for memorization. This work has served as the standard guide for Quran students and teachers for over seven centuries.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A comprehensive Shafi'i fiqh manual that serves as one of the primary references in the Shafi'i school. An-Nawawi abridged and refined ar-Rafi'i's al-Aziz, correcting and selecting the strongest positions. The work covers all chapters of Islamic jurisprudence from purification to inheritance.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A detailed commentary on an-Nawawi's Minhaj at-Talibin and one of the most authoritative texts in Shafi'i jurisprudence. Ibn Hajar al-Haytami explains the legal reasoning behind each ruling, discusses variant opinions, and provides extensive evidence from the Quran, Sunnah, and earlier scholars.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A major commentary on Kanz ad-Daqa'iq in Hanafi jurisprudence. Ibn Nujaym provides clear explanations with strong evidence and discusses practical applications. The work is valued for its precision and is a standard reference in Egyptian and Levantine Hanafi scholarship.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A masterpiece of Hanafi jurisprudence organized by legal principles rather than the traditional chapter format. Al-Kasani rearranged his teacher's work to show how rulings derive from underlying legal reasoning. The systematic approach makes it uniquely valuable for understanding the logic of Hanafi fiqh.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The most important work of Zahiri (literalist) jurisprudence. Ibn Hazm examines every major issue of Islamic law, presenting evidence from the Quran and Sunnah and critiquing the positions of the Hanafi, Maliki, and Shafi'i schools. Known for its rigorous hadith-based methodology and sharp argumentation.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A commentary on Muntaqa al-Akhbar by Ibn Taymiyyah's grandfather, covering the hadith evidence for legal rulings. Ash-Shawkani analyzes each hadith's chain and meaning, compares the positions of the four madhabs, and often favors the strongest evidence regardless of school affiliation.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A concise primer on Hanafi jurisprudence covering worship (purification, prayer, fasting, zakat, hajj). Popular as a beginner's text in traditional Islamic education, it presents the essential Hanafi positions clearly and systematically. Widely taught in madrasas across the Muslim world.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The author's own commentary on his Nur al-Idah, expanding on each ruling with evidence, scholarly reasoning, and practical details. Widely used as an intermediate Hanafi fiqh text in traditional study circles and seminaries.

subject: fiqh
description_en: An extremely concise summary of Hanafi jurisprudence that became one of the most commented-upon texts in the school. Despite its brevity, it covers all major areas of fiqh. Its precision attracted dozens of commentaries, the most famous being al-Bahr ar-Ra'iq by Ibn Nujaym.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A detailed gloss on ad-Dardir's Sharh al-Kabir, which is itself a commentary on Khalil's Mukhtasar. This layered work is a primary reference in Maliki jurisprudence, especially in Egypt and North Africa. Ad-Dasuqi clarifies difficult passages and reconciles apparent contradictions.

subject: tafsir
description_en: One of the most widely studied tafsirs in the Islamic world, especially in Ottoman-era and Turkish-speaking scholarly circles. Al-Baydawi combined the linguistic insights of az-Zamakhshari's al-Kashshaf with orthodox Ash'ari theology, creating a balanced interpretation that has been taught for centuries.

subject: tafsir
description_en: A concise yet comprehensive tafsir that draws from az-Zamakhshari and al-Baydawi while maintaining Maturidi theology. Popular in Hanafi scholarly circles, especially in Central Asia and the Indian subcontinent. Known for its clarity and balance between brevity and substance.

subject: tafsir
description_en: A literary and theological masterpiece of Quranic exegesis by the Grand Mufti of the Ottoman Empire. Abu as-Su'ud combined deep linguistic analysis with Maturidi theology and Hanafi jurisprudence. The work is particularly valued for its rhetorical analysis and attention to the Quran's literary beauty.

subject: tafsir
description_en: One of the last great classical tafsirs, bringing together the insights of previous exegetes while adding the author's own analysis. Al-Alusi, the Mufti of Baghdad, engaged with earlier works like ar-Razi, al-Baydawi, and Abu as-Su'ud, creating a comprehensive synthesis that addresses linguistic, theological, and legal dimensions.

subject: hadith
description_en: A comprehensive hadith commentary that selects narrations from the six major collections and others, organizing them by topic with legal analysis. Al-Baghawi explains the meanings, extracts rulings, and notes differences of opinion among the madhabs. A valuable intermediate work between hadith and fiqh.

subject: hadith
description_en: The largest of at-Tabarani's three mu'jam collections, organizing hadith by the names of the companion narrators in alphabetical order. Contains thousands of narrations, many not found in the six major collections. A critical source for hadith scholars seeking comprehensive evidence and alternative chains.

subject: hadith
description_en: An encyclopedic collection organizing hadith according to the 'branches of faith,' based on the Prophet's statement that faith has over seventy branches. Al-Bayhaqi collected narrations related to every aspect of faith and practice, from belief in Allah to removing harm from the road.

subject: hadith
description_en: One of the most important hadith collections for legal purposes, organizing narrations by jurisprudential chapters. Al-Bayhaqi included hadith from all major and minor collections with his critical analysis of chains. Particularly important for Shafi'i fiqh as it provides the hadith basis for many Shafi'i positions.

subject: hadith
description_en: A collection of narrations and reports about the asceticism and spiritual practices of the Prophet, his companions, and the early generations. Imam Ahmad compiled stories of their devotion, simplicity, weeping from fear of Allah, and detachment from worldly pleasures. An important early work on Islamic spirituality.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: The most widely taught creedal text in North and West Africa. As-Sanusi presents the rational and necessary attributes of Allah in a systematic format accessible to beginners. The text, with its numerous commentaries, forms the backbone of traditional theological education in the Maliki-Ash'ari tradition.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: Al-Ghazali's mature work on Islamic theology, presenting the Ash'ari creed with rational proofs in a balanced and moderate tone. The title means 'moderation in belief,' reflecting al-Ghazali's approach of finding the middle path between excessive rationalism and blind imitation.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: An advanced work on Ash'ari theology by al-Ghazali's teacher, known as Imam al-Haramayn. Al-Juwayni presents decisive proofs for core theological positions including the existence of God, prophethood, and eschatology. A foundational text for advanced students of Islamic theology.

subject: ethics
description_en: One of the most influential manuals of Sufism, grounding spiritual practice firmly in orthodox Sunni Islam. Al-Qushayri begins with biographies of early Sufi masters, then systematically defines key spiritual concepts (stations and states) with evidence from the Quran, Sunnah, and the practice of the righteous predecessors.

subject: ethics
description_en: A collection of spiritual aphorisms that has become one of the most beloved and widely studied texts in Islamic spirituality. Each hikma (wisdom) is a concentrated insight about the relationship between the servant and Allah. Numerous commentaries have been written on it, the most famous by Ibn Abbad ar-Rundi and Ahmad Zarruq.

subject: ethics
description_en: Al-Ghazali's guide for worshippers, outlining the seven valleys or stages that a servant must traverse on the path to Allah: knowledge, repentance, obstacles, motivations, hindrances, praise and gratitude, and love. Written as a practical manual for those seeking spiritual advancement.

subject: ethics
description_en: One of the earliest systematic works on spiritual psychology in Islam. Al-Muhasibi (whose name means 'the one who examines himself') discusses how to guard one's inner states, purify intentions, and recognize the subtle tricks of the ego (nafs). Imam Ahmad initially criticized al-Muhasibi but later acknowledged his sincerity.

subject: ethics
description_en: A comprehensive work on the etiquettes of both worldly and religious life. Al-Mawardi discusses the ethics of knowledge-seeking, the duties of rulers and subjects, the principles of good character, and the balance between pursuing worldly success and preparing for the Hereafter.

subject: ethics
description_en: A delightful collection of wisdom, anecdotes, and etiquettes compiled by the great hadith scholar Ibn Hibban. The work covers topics such as intelligence, patience, silence, speech, friendship, generosity, and humility, drawing from hadith, poetry, and the sayings of the wise.

subject: arabic
description_en: The most popular commentary on Ibn Malik's Alfiyyah (the thousand-line poem on Arabic grammar). Ibn Aqil explains each verse with clear examples, making the complex grammar rules accessible to intermediate students. This commentary has been the standard textbook for Arabic grammar in traditional Islamic education for centuries.

subject: arabic
description_en: A groundbreaking work on Arabic linguistic theory and the philosophy of language. Ibn Jinni explores the nature of language, its origins, the relationship between sound and meaning, and the principles governing linguistic change. Considered one of the most original works in Arabic linguistics.

subject: arabic
description_en: A foundational work on Arabic rhetoric and the inimitability (i'jaz) of the Quran. Al-Jurjani developed the theory of nazm (arrangement), arguing that the Quran's miracle lies in the unique arrangement of its words and meanings. This work revolutionized Arabic literary criticism and rhetorical theory.

subject: arabic
description_en: A companion work to Dala'il al-I'jaz, focusing on the figures of speech (majaz, isti'arah, tashbih) in Arabic. Al-Jurjani analyzes metaphor, simile, and other literary devices with examples from the Quran, hadith, and Arabic poetry, establishing the theoretical framework for Arabic rhetoric (balaghah).

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: One of the most important and comprehensive works on Islamic legal theory (usul al-fiqh). Ar-Razi synthesized the approaches of al-Ghazali's al-Mustasfa and al-Amidi's al-Ihkam, creating a magisterial treatment of the sources of law, linguistic analysis, legal reasoning, and the principles of ijtihad.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: A masterwork of Islamic legal theory that became a standard advanced textbook. Al-Amidi presents the principles of jurisprudence with exceptional logical rigor, examining the Quran, Sunnah, consensus, analogy, and other sources of law. Known for its thorough analysis of legal disputes between the schools.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: Ibn Hazm's articulation of Zahiri legal methodology, rejecting qiyas (analogy) and emphasizing the apparent meaning of texts. A brilliant and controversial work that challenges the legal theories of the other schools with sharp logic and vast textual evidence. Essential reading for understanding Islamic legal pluralism.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: A unique work in Islamic jurisprudence that identifies and explains the subtle distinctions between similar-looking legal cases that receive different rulings. Al-Qarafi presents 548 such distinctions, demonstrating the depth and precision of Islamic legal reasoning. Essential for advanced fiqh students.

subject: hadith_sciences
description_en: One of the earliest systematic works on hadith methodology, classifying the sciences of hadith into 52 categories. Al-Hakim an-Naysaburi (author of al-Mustadrak) defines each type of hadith with examples and explains the criteria for authentication. A foundational text in the development of hadith sciences.

subject: hadith_sciences
description_en: A comprehensive treatise on the principles of hadith transmission and narrator criticism. Al-Khatib al-Baghdadi, one of the greatest hadith scholars of Baghdad, covers the conditions for accepting narrators, the rules for hearing and transmitting hadith, and the methodology of the hadith masters.

subject: hadith_sciences
description_en: A work on the etiquettes and ethics of hadith narrators and students of hadith. Al-Khatib covers the manners of seeking knowledge, the responsibilities of teachers and students, proper behavior during hadith sessions, and the moral qualities required of those who carry the Prophet's legacy.

subject: seerah
description_en: Ibn Kathir's biography of the Prophet, extracted from his larger work al-Bidayah wan-Nihayah. It combines the narrative approach of earlier seerah works with Ibn Kathir's rigorous hadith methodology, critically evaluating the chains of narration for each incident. A scholarly yet readable account of the Prophet's life.

subject: seerah
description_en: The most comprehensive biography of the Prophet Muhammad ever compiled, spanning twelve volumes. As-Salihi gathered virtually every authentic report about the Prophet's life, characteristics, miracles, battles, and companions. The sheer volume of material makes it an indispensable reference for seerah scholars.

subject: history
description_en: A foundational work on the early Islamic conquests, covering the expansion from the Prophet's time through the Umayyad period. Al-Baladhuri provides detailed accounts of each campaign with information about treaties, administration, land distribution, and the establishment of garrison cities. Essential for understanding early Islamic history.

subject: history
description_en: A sweeping world history and geography by 'the Herodotus of the Arabs.' Al-Mas'udi covers the history of nations from creation to his own time, including descriptions of lands, peoples, customs, and scientific knowledge. Written in an engaging narrative style that combines historical fact with cultural observation.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The first systematic treatise on Islamic constitutional law and political governance. Al-Mawardi discusses the caliphate, its conditions and appointment, the duties of ministers, governors, judges, and military commanders, as well as taxation and public administration. A foundational text for Islamic political thought.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The most important Maliki work on Islamic judicial procedure. Ibn Farhun covers the qualifications of judges, courtroom procedure, the evaluation of evidence and testimony, oaths, documentation, and the administration of justice. An essential reference for understanding the Islamic judicial system.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: A revolutionary work on the objectives of Islamic law (maqasid ash-shariah) that shifted legal theory from focusing solely on textual evidence to considering the higher purposes behind divine legislation. Ash-Shatibi identified five essential objectives: preservation of religion, life, intellect, lineage, and property.

subject: hadith_sciences
description_en: The most influential intermediate work on hadith terminology and methodology, synthesizing the earlier works of al-Hakim and al-Khatib into a comprehensive textbook. Nearly every subsequent work on hadith sciences was either a commentary, summary, or expansion of this text. An-Nawawi's Taqrib is its most famous abridgment.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A masterful commentary on al-Hidayah that combines hadith verification with legal analysis. Ibn al-Humam, one of the greatest late Hanafi scholars, brings the methods of the hadith scholars to bear on Hanafi jurisprudence, strengthening or qualifying rulings based on the strength of their evidence.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A systematic presentation of the maxims and principles underlying Hanafi jurisprudence. Ibn Nujaym identifies the five universal maxims of Islamic law and derives subsidiary principles, showing how individual rulings connect to broader legal reasoning. A key text for understanding Islamic legal theory in practice.

title_ar: الوجيز في فقه السنة والكتاب العزيز
subject: fiqh
description_en: A modern comparative fiqh manual that presents rulings based directly on Quran and Sunnah evidence, noting where the madhabs agree and differ. Written in an accessible style for contemporary readers, it covers all major areas of worship and transactions with clear evidence-based reasoning.

title_ar: الإقناع في حل ألفاظ أبي شجاع
subject: fiqh
description_en: A clear and detailed commentary on the popular Shafi'i primer Matn Abu Shuja (al-Ghayah wat-Taqrib). Ash-Shirbini explains each concise phrase with the reasoning behind rulings, making it ideal for intermediate students of Shafi'i jurisprudence. Widely used in Southeast Asian Islamic education.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A comprehensive commentary on an-Nawawi's Minhaj at-Talibin, and one of the most relied-upon references in Shafi'i jurisprudence. Ash-Shirbini's clear prose and thorough analysis make it the preferred commentary for many scholars, alongside Tuhfat al-Muhtaj by Ibn Hajar al-Haytami.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The third great commentary on an-Nawawi's Minhaj, completing the trio with Tuhfat al-Muhtaj and Mughni al-Muhtaj. Ar-Ramli (known as ash-Shafi'i as-Saghir, 'the little Shafi'i') provides meticulous legal analysis. When Egyptian Shafi'i scholars cite 'the relied-upon opinion,' they typically follow ar-Ramli.

subject: quran_sciences
description_en: A work dedicated to extracting legal rulings from the Quran, organizing the ayat by jurisprudential topic. As-Suyuti shows how scholars derive fiqh rulings from Quranic verses, demonstrating the connection between tafsir and fiqh. A unique contribution to both Quranic studies and legal methodology.

subject: hadith
description_en: An encyclopedic commentary on the hadith in Imam Malik's al-Muwatta, organized by the names of narrators. Ibn Abd al-Barr provides the chains of transmission, grades each hadith, explains its legal implications, and discusses the views of scholars from all madhabs. A monumental achievement in hadith scholarship.

subject: hadith
description_en: A companion work to at-Tamhid, this commentary on al-Muwatta is organized by topic rather than by narrator. Ibn Abd al-Barr covers the jurisprudential issues raised by each hadith, presenting the positions of all schools with their evidence. Together with at-Tamhid, it forms the most comprehensive treatment of al-Muwatta.

subject: hadith_sciences
description_en: An abridgment and refinement of al-Mizzi's Tahdhib al-Kamal, the comprehensive biographical dictionary of hadith narrators. Ibn Hajar condensed the material while adding critical evaluations and additional biographical data not found in the original. A standard reference for hadith narrator criticism (ilm ar-rijal).

subject: hadith_sciences
description_en: A concise summary of Tahdhib at-Tahdhib, providing the essential biographical data and grading for each narrator in one or two lines. Ibn Hajar assigns each narrator a grade from 'sahabi' (highest) down to various levels of reliability or weakness. Indispensable for quick reference in hadith authentication.

subject: history
description_en: A biographical dictionary of the scholars, hadith narrators, jurists, and notable figures who lived in or visited Baghdad. Al-Khatib's work is invaluable for Islamic intellectual history, providing details about the lives, scholarship, and interconnections of thousands of scholars during Baghdad's golden age.

subject: history
description_en: An extensive genealogical and biographical history of the noble Arab families, particularly those connected to the Prophet and early Islam. Al-Baladhuri traces the lineages of the Quraysh, the Ansar, and the prominent companions, providing rich historical detail about the formative period of Islam.

subject: fiqh
description_en: Known as Mukhtasar al-Quduri, this is the most widely memorized and taught primer of Hanafi jurisprudence worldwide. Its concise text covers all essential rulings in worship and transactions. Countless commentaries have been written on it, and it remains the first text studied in many Hanafi seminaries.

subject: general
description_en: The complete collection of Ibn Taymiyyah's legal opinions, theological treatises, and letters, compiled posthumously by his students. Covering creed, jurisprudence, Sufism, logic, philosophy, and comparative religion across 37 volumes, it represents one of the most extensive scholarly outputs in Islamic history.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: An advanced work on Islamic theology that became a standard textbook in Ottoman-era madrasas. At-Taftazani covers the major theological questions with rigorous logical argumentation from the Maturidi-Ash'ari perspective. The work addresses existence of God, divine attributes, prophethood, and eschatology with philosophical depth.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: A widely studied commentary on Najm ad-Din an-Nasafi's creedal text, representing the standard Maturidi theological position. At-Taftazani explains each article of faith with rational proofs and responds to deviant positions. This text, with its super-commentaries, formed the core of theological education across the Ottoman world.

subject: biography
description_en: The most comprehensive biographical dictionary of Shafi'i scholars, from Imam ash-Shafi'i himself through the author's contemporaries. As-Subki provides detailed biographies, scholarly evaluations, and anecdotes about hundreds of jurists, hadith scholars, and theologians who followed the Shafi'i school.

subject: biography
description_en: The most important biographical dictionary of Hanafi scholars, covering the lives and works of jurists from the school's founding through the 8th century AH. Al-Qurashi traces the transmission of Hanafi scholarship across generations and regions, documenting the school's intellectual heritage.

subject: biography
description_en: The standard biographical dictionary of Maliki scholars, covering the lives and contributions of jurists from Imam Malik through the 8th century AH. Ibn Farhun documents the spread of the Maliki school across North Africa, al-Andalus, Egypt, and other regions with detailed scholarly profiles.

subject: fiqh
description_en: An introduction to the Hanbali school of jurisprudence covering its methodology, terminology, major scholars, key texts, and the development of the school from Imam Ahmad through modern times. A valuable guide for students beginning their study of Hanbali fiqh and its rich scholarly tradition.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: The first systematic work on Islamic legal theory ever written. Ash-Shafi'i laid out the hierarchy of legal sources (Quran, Sunnah, ijma, qiyas) and defined the methodology for deriving rulings. This revolutionary work created usul al-fiqh as a distinct discipline and influenced all subsequent legal thought.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A concise guide to the areas of consensus (ijma) and disagreement (ikhtilaf) among the four Sunni schools of jurisprudence. Ibn Qudamah presents each issue systematically, noting where all four madhabs agree and where they differ, with evidence for each position.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: A widely studied primer on Shafi'i legal theory by the first teacher at the Nizamiyyah college in Baghdad. Ash-Shirazi presents the principles of jurisprudence in a clear, logical format that makes it ideal for intermediate students of Islamic law.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A comprehensive Shafi'i fiqh text that an-Nawawi later chose as the basis for his monumental al-Majmu commentary. Ash-Shirazi's clear presentation of Shafi'i positions with their evidence made this work a standard reference for centuries.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The most popular Shafi'i primer in the world, memorized by students across Southeast Asia, East Africa, and the Middle East. This extremely concise text covers the essential rulings of worship, transactions, and personal status in a few dozen pages, serving as the entry point to Shafi'i jurisprudence.

subject: fiqh
description_en: An intermediate-level Hanbali fiqh text that became the foundation for advanced study. Ibn Qudamah presents the primary positions of the Hanbali school concisely, and the work attracted several important commentaries including al-Insaf by al-Mardawi.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A commentary on Umdat al-Fiqh, the beginner-level Hanbali text by Ibn Qudamah's uncle. This clear and well-organized commentary is ideal for students beginning their study of Hanbali jurisprudence, explaining the evidence and reasoning behind each ruling.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: A comprehensive collection of Islamic legal maxims (qawa'id fiqhiyyah) organized alphabetically. Az-Zarkashi gathered maxims from the Shafi'i school showing how general principles govern specific rulings. Essential for understanding how Islamic law operates through broad legal reasoning.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: A masterwork of Shafi'i legal theory by al-Ghazali's teacher, Imam al-Haramayn al-Juwayni. Known for its depth and originality, this work introduced new approaches to legal reasoning including early discussions of maqasid (objectives) of Islamic law that influenced later scholars like ash-Shatibi.

subject: ethics
description_en: A beloved collection of sermons and exhortations on faith, worship, and moral conduct. As-Samarqandi draws on Quranic verses, hadith, and stories of the righteous to awaken heedless hearts. Widely read in mosques and study circles across the Muslim world for over a millennium.

subject: ethics
description_en: A practical guide identifying and explaining the major sins in Islam, drawing on Quranic warnings and prophetic traditions. Adh-Dhahabi lists each sin with its evidence and consequences, making it an important reference for Muslims seeking to avoid the gravest transgressions.

subject: tafsir
description_en: A popular and accessible commentary based primarily on Tafsir al-Baghawi, simplified for a wider audience. Al-Khazin removes the hadith chains and focuses on meaning, making it particularly suitable for general readers and students beginning their study of tafsir.

subject: tafsir
description_en: A modern, accessible commentary on the entire Quran designed for the general Muslim reader. Al-Jaza'iri explains each passage in clear, contemporary Arabic with practical lessons and guidance. One of the most widely distributed modern tafsirs.

subject: tafsir
description_en: The most comprehensive modern Urdu commentary on the Quran, based on classical sources especially Tafsir Ibn Kathir and the Hanafi jurisprudential tradition. Mufti Shafi Usmani addresses contemporary issues while remaining grounded in traditional scholarship. Translated into English as an 8-volume set.

subject: hadith
description_en: A comprehensive collection of authentic supplications and remembrances (adhkar) for every occasion in a Muslim's daily life, from waking up to sleeping, eating, traveling, and worship. An-Nawawi graded each hadith and organized them by situation, making it the most popular book of its kind.

author_name_en: Sa'id ibn Ali ibn Wahf al-Qahtani
subject: hadith
description_en: A pocket-sized collection of authentic daily supplications and remembrances extracted from the Quran and Sunnah. Despite its small size, it has become one of the most widely distributed Islamic books in history, translated into dozens of languages and carried by millions of Muslims worldwide.

subject: biography
description_en: A comprehensive biography of the Prophet's companions organized by theme, drawing from authentic hadith sources. Al-Kandhalwi presents the sacrifices, worship, brotherhood, and courage of the Sahaba as a model for modern Muslims. Widely read in Tablighi Jamaat circles and Islamic study groups worldwide.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A foundational work in Maliki jurisprudence by the grandfather of the famous philosopher Ibn Rushd. It serves as an extensive introduction to the Mudawwanah, explaining the principles behind Maliki rulings and providing essential context for understanding the school's methodology.

subject: fiqh
