description_en: A massive Shafi'i fiqh encyclopedia that comments on al-Muzani's Mukhtasar. Al-Mawardi's work is notable for its thoroughness in presenting all opinions within the Shafi'i school and from other schools, with extensive legal reasoning. It remains a key reference for advanced Shafi'i scholarship.

title_en: Sharh Sahih al-Bukhari by Ibn Battal
subject: hadith
description_en: One of the earliest and most important commentaries on Sahih al-Bukhari, predating Fath al-Bari by four centuries. Ibn Battal focuses on extracting legal rulings from the hadith, presenting the views of the major schools of fiqh. Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani frequently referenced this work in his own commentary.

subject: hadith
description_en: A comprehensive commentary on Sahih al-Bukhari written from a Hanafi perspective, considered the counterpart to Ibn Hajar's Fath al-Bari. Al-Ayni, a contemporary and scholarly rival of Ibn Hajar, provides detailed linguistic analysis and Hanafi legal reasoning throughout.

subject: hadith
description_en: A major commentary on Sahih Muslim by the renowned Maliki judge Qadi Iyad of Ceuta. The work explains hadith meanings, extracts legal rulings, and discusses the views of the Maliki school. An-Nawawi later built upon this work in his own commentary on Sahih Muslim.

subject: arabic
description_en: A foundational text on Arabic rhetoric (balaghah) that systematized the three branches: ma'ani (meanings), bayan (clarity), and badi' (embellishment). As-Sakkaki's classification became the standard framework for studying Arabic rhetoric and remains the basis for balaghah curricula in Islamic seminaries.

subject: arabic
description_en: A concise and elegant summary of As-Sakkaki's Miftah al-Ulum, focusing on the third section on rhetoric. Al-Qazwini's abridgment became more popular than the original and attracted dozens of commentaries. It remains the most widely studied text on Arabic balaghah in the Islamic world.

subject: arabic
description_en: A widely studied intermediate Arabic grammar text and its commentary by the same author. Ibn Hisham explains Arabic syntax with clear examples from the Quran, hadith, and poetry. It bridges the gap between beginner texts like al-Ajrumiyyah and advanced works like Alfiyyat Ibn Malik.

subject: arabic
description_en: The most important work on Arabic grammar for Quranic parsing and understanding. Ibn Hisham organizes his discussion of Arabic particles and syntactic structures with extensive Quranic examples, making it an essential reference for anyone studying the language of the Quran at an advanced level.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The most comprehensive collection of Imam ash-Shafi'i's jurisprudential opinions, compiled from his teachings in Egypt (his 'new' school). It contains his detailed legal reasoning on every major topic, debates with scholars from other schools, and forms the primary source for understanding Shafi'i methodology firsthand.

subject: biography
description_en: A comprehensive biographical dictionary of the Prophet's companions, listing over 3,500 entries. Ibn Abd al-Barr provides names, lineages, conversion dates, and key narrations for each companion. Ibn Hajar later wrote al-Isabah partly to supplement and correct this work.

subject: biography
description_en: A major biographical dictionary of the companions that synthesized and improved upon the earlier works of Ibn Abd al-Barr and Abu Nu'aym. Ibn al-Athir arranged entries alphabetically and added critical analysis. Together with al-Isabah, it forms the definitive reference for companion studies.

subject: biography
description_en: An extensive collection of biographies of the righteous, from the companions through the tabi'in and later scholars. Abu Nu'aym records their worship, asceticism, knowledge, and spiritual states with full chains of narration. A treasure trove of early Islamic spiritual and scholarly culture.

title_ar: الفكر السامي في تاريخ الفقه الإسلامي
subject: history
description_en: A comprehensive history of Islamic jurisprudence from its origins to the modern period. Al-Hajwi traces the development of fiqh through its major phases: the prophetic era, the companions, the formation of the schools, the period of commentary, and modern reform movements. Essential for understanding how Islamic law evolved.

subject: ethics
description_en: A concise and beautiful letter from al-Ghazali to a student, summarizing the essence of the spiritual path: knowledge without action is madness, and action without sincerity is useless. This short treatise distills al-Ghazali's mature wisdom into practical advice that remains deeply relevant.

subject: ethics
description_en: A Persian abridgment of the Ihya Ulum ad-Din, written for a broader audience. Al-Ghazali distills the key teachings of his magnum opus into a more accessible form, covering self-knowledge, knowledge of God, knowledge of the world, and knowledge of the hereafter.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: A groundbreaking work on Islamic legal theory that emphasizes the objectives and spirit of the law. Ibn al-Qayyim argues that Islamic law is built upon wisdom and justice, and that legal rulings must serve the welfare of people. He provides hundreds of examples of how the Prophet and early scholars applied flexible reasoning.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A specialized work on Islamic judicial policy (siyasah shar'iyyah) and the methods of determining truth in legal proceedings. Ibn al-Qayyim discusses circumstantial evidence, expert testimony, and investigative techniques that judges may use, showing that Islamic law accommodates sophisticated evidentiary reasoning.

title_ar: شفاء العليل في مسائل القضاء والقدر
subject: aqeedah
description_en: A detailed treatment of divine decree (qadr) and predestination in Islamic theology. Ibn al-Qayyim addresses the philosophical and theological questions surrounding free will, divine knowledge, and human responsibility, presenting the Sunni position with clarity and depth.

subject: general
description_en: Extracted from Zad al-Ma'ad, this work compiles the Prophet's guidance on health, diet, and medicine. Ibn al-Qayyim discusses remedies mentioned in hadith (honey, black seed, cupping) alongside general principles of wellness, preventive care, and the relationship between spiritual and physical health.

subject: arabic
description_en: A masterpiece of Arabic prose literature on the art of rhetoric, eloquence, and public speaking. Al-Jahiz combines linguistic theory with entertaining anecdotes, showcasing the highest examples of Arabic eloquence. It remains one of the most celebrated works of Arabic literary criticism and adab literature.

subject: seerah
description_en: A detailed commentary on Ibn Hisham's Sirah that provides linguistic explanations, historical context, genealogies, and legal insights not found in the original. As-Suhayli's encyclopedic knowledge enriches every page, making this the most important classical commentary on the Prophet's biography.

subject: seerah
description_en: A comprehensive biography of the Prophet focusing on his unique qualities, miracles, and the blessings associated with him. Al-Qastallani drew from hundreds of sources to create a work that celebrates the Prophet's character and spiritual station. Az-Zurqani's commentary on it became equally famous.

title_ar: كشف الظنون عن أسامي الكتب والفنون
subject: general
description_en: The most important Islamic bibliography ever compiled, cataloguing approximately 15,000 books and treatises in Arabic, Persian, and Turkish. Hajji Khalifah lists each work with its author, subject, and often a brief description. An indispensable reference for understanding the scope of Islamic literary heritage.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The most comprehensive modern comparative fiqh encyclopedia. Az-Zuhayli covers every area of Islamic law, presenting the views of all four Sunni schools with their evidence, then discussing modern applications. This work bridges classical scholarship and contemporary needs, widely used in universities and courts.

subject: hadith
description_en: Imam Muhammad's transmission of Imam Malik's al-Muwatta with his own additions and Hanafi commentary. After each hadith, Muhammad ash-Shaybani notes whether the Hanafi school agrees or differs from Malik's position, making it a unique work that bridges the two great schools of early Islamic law.

title_ar: الجواب الصحيح لمن بدل دين المسيح
subject: comparative_religion
description_en: The most extensive classical Islamic response to Christianity, written in reply to a letter from a Cypriot Christian. Ibn Taymiyyah examines Christian scriptures, the Trinity, the divinity of Jesus, and the integrity of biblical texts, providing detailed theological arguments from an Islamic perspective.

subject: comparative_religion
description_en: Written after a famous public debate between the author and a Christian missionary in India, this work examines the textual integrity of the Bible and argues for the truth of Islam. Al-Kairanawi's scholarship and use of European biblical criticism made this one of the most influential Islamic apologetic works.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: The most important modern work on the objectives of Islamic law, building on ash-Shatibi's foundation. Ibn Ashur argues for making maqasid an independent discipline of Islamic law, not merely a subsidiary of usul al-fiqh. His work renewed scholarly interest in purpose-based legal reasoning.

subject: tafsir
description_en: A monumental modern tafsir that combines classical scholarship with maqasid-based analysis. Ibn Ashur pays special attention to the Quran's linguistic beauty, literary structure, and higher purposes. His unique approach of examining each surah's overarching themes makes this work distinctive among 20th-century commentaries.

A comprehensive manual of Shafi'i jurisprudence by Imam al-Nawawi, widely used as a primary reference text in the Shafi'i school. It covers all major areas of Islamic law from purification to inheritance.
subject: fiqh

description_en: A detailed commentary on Kanz al-Daqaiq by al-Nasafi, one of the most important Hanafi legal texts. Al-Zayla'i provides thorough analysis of legal rulings with evidence from hadith and the opinions of early Hanafi scholars.
subject: fiqh

description_en: A gloss (hashiyah) on Tafsir al-Jalalayn, providing additional commentary and clarification on the concise Quranic exegesis. It became one of the most studied supplementary tafsir works in traditional Islamic education.
subject: tafsir

title_ar: الإقناع في حل ألفاظ أبي شجاع
title_en: Al-Iqna fi Hall Alfaz Abi Shuja
description_en: A detailed commentary on the Matn of Abu Shuja (Ghayat al-Taqrib), a foundational Shafi'i fiqh text. Al-Shirbini's explanation makes the concise matn accessible to students of Islamic jurisprudence.
subject: fiqh

description_en: The most comprehensive encyclopedia of Hanbali jurisprudence and one of the greatest works of Islamic comparative law. Ibn Qudamah systematically presents the Hanbali position alongside other schools, providing evidence-based analysis.
subject: fiqh

description_en: An abridgment of al-Rafi'i's Fath al-Aziz, this is one of the most authoritative references in Shafi'i jurisprudence. Al-Nawawi refined and corrected the positions, making it a standard reference for Shafi'i scholars.
subject: fiqh

description_en: A major commentary on al-Nawawi's Minhaj al-Talibin, considered one of the two most authoritative references in late Shafi'i jurisprudence alongside Tuhfat al-Muhtaj. Al-Ramli is known as "al-Shafi'i al-Saghir" (the Little Shafi'i).
subject: fiqh

description_en: One of the two most authoritative commentaries on al-Nawawi's Minhaj al-Talibin, alongside Nihayat al-Muhtaj. Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's work is particularly followed in the Hadrami, Southeast Asian, and Egyptian Shafi'i traditions.
subject: fiqh

description_en: A foundational Shafi'i legal text that became one of the most commented-upon works in Islamic jurisprudence. Al-Nawawi's famous al-Majmu' is a commentary on this work.
subject: fiqh

title_ar: الوجيز في فقه مذهب الإمام الشافعي
description_en: A concise manual of Shafi'i jurisprudence by the great scholar al-Ghazali. It became the basis for al-Rafi'i's monumental Fath al-Aziz commentary, which in turn was abridged by al-Nawawi.
subject: fiqh

description_en: A major commentary on Mukhtasar Khalil, the most important Maliki legal compendium. Al-Dardir's explanation is one of the most studied texts in the Maliki school, especially in North and West Africa.
subject: fiqh

description_en: A detailed gloss on al-Dardir's Sharh al-Kabir, serving as one of the primary reference works in the Maliki school. Al-Dasuqi provides additional analysis and helps resolve conflicting opinions within the school.
subject: fiqh

description_en: The foundational text of Maliki jurisprudence, compiled by Sahnun from the teachings of Ibn al-Qasim, a direct student of Imam Malik. It records Imam Malik's legal opinions on thousands of questions and became the primary source for the Maliki school.
subject: fiqh

description_en: The base text upon which al-Marginani built his famous al-Hidayah commentary. It combines the content of al-Quduri's Mukhtasar with al-Shaybani's al-Jami al-Saghir into a single concise Hanafi legal primer.
subject: fiqh

description_en: A masterful commentary on al-Hidayah, considered one of the finest works of Hanafi legal analysis. Ibn al-Humam combines deep knowledge of hadith with rigorous legal reasoning, examining evidence from all schools.
subject: fiqh

description_en: The most authoritative late Hanafi legal reference, commonly known as "Hashiyat Ibn Abidin." It is a massive commentary on al-Haskafi's al-Durr al-Mukhtar and remains the primary reference for Hanafi fatwa-giving worldwide.
subject: fiqh

title_ar: الإنصاف في معرفة الراجح من الخلاف
description_en: The definitive work for determining the preferred opinion within the Hanbali school. Al-Mardawi catalogues and weighs the various narrations and positions attributed to Imam Ahmad, making it indispensable for Hanbali scholarship.
subject: fiqh

description_en: A standard Hanbali fiqh manual that became one of the most authoritative mu'tamad texts in the school. Al-Buhuti's Kashshaf al-Qina' is the celebrated commentary on this work.
subject: fiqh

description_en: The most important commentary on al-Hajjawi's al-Iqna', and one of the two most authoritative references in late Hanbali jurisprudence. It is particularly followed in Saudi Arabia and the Gulf states.
subject: fiqh

description_en: One of the most comprehensive works in Hanbali jurisprudence, covering the various narrations from Imam Ahmad. Ibn Muflih was a student of Ibn Taymiyyah and his work became a major reference for the school.
subject: fiqh

description_en: Al-Buhuti's commentary on Ibn al-Najjar's Muntaha al-Iradat, the other of the two central Hanbali references alongside Kashshaf al-Qina'. These two works together form the backbone of authoritative Hanbali legal opinion.
subject: fiqh

description_en: An introduction to the al-Mudawwanah by the grandfather of the famous philosopher Ibn Rushd. It provides essential context and legal principles needed to understand the foundational Maliki text.
subject: fiqh

description_en: A massive commentary on al-Utbiyyah, one of the earliest Maliki legal compilations. Ibn Rushd al-Jadd provides extensive analysis of early Maliki opinions, making it a crucial work for understanding the school's formative period.
subject: fiqh

description_en: One of the most comprehensive works of Maliki jurisprudence, covering the entire range of Islamic law. Al-Qarafi is also famous for his work on legal maxims (al-Furuq) and usul al-fiqh.
subject: fiqh

description_en: The most comprehensive work of Hanafi jurisprudence ever written, dictated by al-Sarakhsi while imprisoned in a well. It is a commentary on al-Shaybani's works and covers every area of Islamic law in exhaustive detail.
subject: fiqh

description_en: A masterpiece of Hanafi jurisprudence that reorganizes fiqh by topic rather than following the traditional chapter order. Al-Kasani's systematic approach and clear reasoning made it one of the most appreciated Hanafi legal works.
subject: fiqh

description_en: The magnum opus of Zahiri (literalist) jurisprudence, covering all areas of Islamic law. Ibn Hazm argues every point with direct evidence from Quran and Sunnah, rejecting analogical reasoning (qiyas) and taqlid.
subject: fiqh

title_ar: التمهيد لما في الموطأ من المعاني والأسانيد
description_en: A monumental commentary on Imam Malik's Muwatta, organized by chain of narration. Ibn Abd al-Barr provides extensive hadith criticism, biographical information, and legal analysis from all schools.
subject: hadith

description_en: A companion work to al-Tamhid, organized by legal chapter rather than by chain of narration. Together, these two works constitute the most comprehensive commentary on the Muwatta of Imam Malik.
subject: hadith

description_en: One of the three greatest commentaries on Sahih al-Bukhari, alongside Fath al-Bari. Al-Ayni wrote this as a Hanafi counterpart to Ibn Hajar's Shafi'i-leaning commentary, providing alternative legal interpretations.
subject: hadith

description_en: The third of the three great commentaries on Sahih al-Bukhari. Al-Qastallani synthesizes the insights of both Ibn Hajar and al-Ayni, creating an accessible and comprehensive explanation of the hadith collection.
subject: hadith

description_en: A completion of al-Maziri's al-Mu'allim bi-Fawaid Muslim, providing a comprehensive commentary on Sahih Muslim. Qadi Iyad's work became the foundation for al-Nawawi's later commentary on Sahih Muslim.
subject: hadith

description_en: The most famous commentary on Sahih Muslim, al-Nawawi provides clear explanations of hadith texts, discusses variant readings, explains difficult vocabulary, and presents the legal rulings derived from each narration.
subject: hadith

description_en: A comprehensive commentary on Jami' al-Tirmidhi by the Indian hadith scholar al-Mubarakpuri. It provides detailed explanations of hadith texts, discusses chains of narration, and presents legal opinions from various schools.
subject: hadith

title_ar: عون المعبود شرح سنن أبي داود
description_en: The most well-known commentary on Sunan Abi Dawud. Al-Azimabadi provides hadith grading, explains difficult terms, and discusses the legal implications of each narration with reference to all four major schools.
subject: hadith

description_en: Al-Suyuti's commentary on Sunan al-Nasai, also known as Zahr al-Ruba ala al-Mujtaba. It provides concise explanations of hadiths and their legal implications in the tradition of al-Suyuti's prolific scholarship.
subject: hadith

description_en: Al-Suyuti's commentary on Sunan Ibn Majah, titled Misbah al-Zujajah. Al-Suyuti provides concise annotations clarifying hadith meanings, noting weak narrations, and connecting them to legal rulings.
subject: hadith

description_en: A critical work of hadith verification (takhrij) for the hadiths cited in al-Rafi'i's al-Sharh al-Kabir. Ibn Hajar traces each hadith to its original sources and assesses its authenticity, making it invaluable for Shafi'i legal studies.
subject: hadith

description_en: A groundbreaking work of hadith verification for al-Marginani's al-Hidayah. Al-Zayla'i traces every hadith cited in the most important Hanafi legal text, assesses its authenticity, and provides alternative narrations.
subject: hadith

description_en: The most comprehensive work on hadith narrator biographies in Islamic scholarship. Al-Mizzi refined and expanded al-Maqdisi's earlier Kamal, cataloging thousands of narrators from the six canonical hadith collections.
subject: hadith

description_en: An abridgment and refinement of al-Mizzi's Tahdhib al-Kamal, with additional critical assessments and corrections. Ibn Hajar's work became the standard quick-reference for hadith narrator criticism.
subject: hadith

description_en: An ultra-concise summary of Tahdhib al-Tahdhib, giving a one-line grading for each narrator in the six books. It became the most practical quick-reference tool for hadith scholars assessing narrator reliability.
subject: hadith

description_en: A reworking of al-Dhahabi's Mizan al-I'tidal focused on weak and discredited hadith narrators. Ibn Hajar adds corrections, additional information, and narrators that al-Dhahabi missed, creating the most complete reference for weak narrators.
subject: hadith

description_en: The most comprehensive biographical dictionary of the Companions of the Prophet Muhammad. Ibn Hajar catalogues over 12,000 Companions, providing their names, lineages, dates, and notable narrations.
subject: seerah

description_en: A major biographical dictionary of the Companions, compiled from earlier works by Ibn Abd al-Barr, Ibn Mandah, and Abu Nu'aym. Ibn al-Athir lists over 7,500 Companions with their biographical information.
subject: seerah

description_en: A massive biographical encyclopedia of the most distinguished figures in Islamic history, from the Companions to al-Dhahabi's contemporaries. It contains biographies of over 5,000 scholars, rulers, and notable figures.
subject: history

description_en: One of the most celebrated biographical dictionaries in Arabic literature. Ibn Khallikan compiled biographies of 865 notable figures, known for its literary elegance and careful attention to dates of death and historical accuracy.
subject: history

description_en: A comprehensive chronicle of world history from creation to al-Dhahabi's era, written from an Islamic perspective. It covers pre-Islamic prophets, the seerah, the Rashidun caliphate, the Umayyads, Abbasids, and beyond.
subject: history

description_en: A pioneering work of world history and geography by the "Herodotus of the Arabs." Al-Masudi combines historical narrative with geography, natural science, and cultural observation in an encyclopedic format.
subject: history

description_en: The full historical work of which the famous Muqaddimah is the introduction. The Kitab al-Ibar covers the history of the Arabs, Persians, Berbers, and other peoples, applying Ibn Khaldun's revolutionary sociological theories.
subject: history
usul_fiqh
description_en: A groundbreaking work on the objectives of Islamic law (maqasid ash-shari'ah). Ash-Shatibi argues that understanding the higher purposes and intents behind legal rulings is essential for proper juristic reasoning. This work revolutionized usul al-fiqh by systematizing the theory of maslahah (public interest) and became foundational for the modern maqasid movement.

subject: quran_sciences
description_en: The most comprehensive classical work on the sciences of the Quran, covering 80 categories including revelation, compilation, recitation styles, linguistic analysis, abrogation, and exegetical methodology. As-Suyuti synthesized the findings of all previous scholars in the field into this encyclopedic reference.

subject: quran_sciences
description_en: A pioneering encyclopedic work on the sciences of the Quran, organized into 47 types of Quranic knowledge. Az-Zarkashi covers topics from the circumstances of revelation to linguistic subtleties, literary analysis, and legal derivation from the Quran. This work directly influenced as-Suyuti's later Al-Itqan.

subject: hadith
description_en: A major hadith collection containing narrations that meet the criteria of Bukhari and Muslim but were not included in their Sahih collections. Al-Hakim compiled approximately 8,800 hadiths with his assessments of their authenticity. Adh-Dhahabi later reviewed the work and provided corrections in his Talkhis.

subject: hadith
description_en: A hadith collection focused on legal hadiths, compiled by one of the greatest hadith critics in Islamic history. Ad-Daraqutni examines chains of narration with unparalleled precision, noting defects and hidden weaknesses. The work is essential for specialists in hadith criticism and comparative fiqh.

subject: hadith
description_en: The largest of at-Tabarani's three mu'jam (dictionary-style hadith) collections, organized alphabetically by the names of Companions who narrated the hadiths. It contains approximately 25,000 narrations and is an invaluable source for hadiths not found in the six canonical collections.

subject: hadith
description_en: The middle-sized of at-Tabarani's three mu'jam collections, organized by the names of his own teachers. It contains approximately 10,000 narrations and is known for preserving many unique (gharib) hadiths not found in other collections. Scholars value it particularly for its rare chains of transmission.

subject: hadith
description_en: A collection of hadith that the author considered authentic (sahih), arranged by fiqh chapters. Ibn Khuzaymah was known as 'the Imam of Imams' for his extraordinary command of hadith. Only about a quarter of the original work survives, but it remains an important source for hadith scholars.

subject: hadith
description_en: A major hadith collection containing narrations that Ibn Hibban authenticated, arranged in a unique organizational system of divisions (taqasim) and types (anwa') rather than the usual fiqh-chapter arrangement. The definitive edition was prepared by Shu'ayb al-Arna'ut. It is one of the most important hadith collections after the six canonical books.

subject: hadith_sciences
description_en: A masterwork of hadith criticism identifying hidden defects (ilal) in narrations. Ad-Daraqutni, considered the greatest hadith critic of his era, examines thousands of hadiths, comparing different chains and wordings to expose subtle errors. This work is indispensable for specialists in ilm al-ilal (the science of hidden defects).

subject: hadith_sciences
description_en: The most comprehensive classical work on weak hadith narrators. Ibn Adi examines approximately 2,200 narrators who were criticized, providing sample narrations from each to demonstrate the nature of their weakness. It is a fundamental reference for the science of narrator criticism (jarh wa ta'dil).

subject: hadith_sciences
description_en: A monumental biographical dictionary of hadith narrators with evaluations of their reliability. Ibn Abi Hatim compiled the judgments of his father Abu Hatim, Abu Zur'ah, and other leading hadith critics on thousands of narrators. It is one of the earliest and most authoritative works in the science of narrator evaluation.

subject: biography
description_en: A biographical dictionary of the greatest hadith memorizers (huffaz) in Islamic history, from the Companions to adh-Dhahabi's own time. Each entry describes the scholar's training, teachers, students, notable works, and rank in hadith memorization. It is a key source for the history of hadith scholarship.

subject: hadith_sciences
description_en: A comprehensive biographical dictionary of hadith narrators who were criticized or disputed. Adh-Dhahabi reviews the judgments of earlier critics with his characteristic balanced approach, often noting when criticism was excessive or when a narrator was trustworthy despite some criticism. It is the most important reference for disputed narrators.

subject: hadith_sciences
description_en: An expansion and correction of adh-Dhahabi's Mizan al-I'tidal. Ibn Hajar adds narrators not found in the Mizan, corrects errors, and provides additional detail on disputed narrators. Together with the Mizan, it forms the most comprehensive reference on criticized hadith narrators.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The most comprehensive work of Hanafi jurisprudence, dictated by as-Sarakhsi to his students while imprisoned in a well. It is an exhaustive commentary on the Mukhtasar of at-Tahawi, covering the full range of Islamic law with detailed reasoning and evidence. It remains the primary reference for detailed Hanafi legal positions.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A major Hanafi fiqh work renowned for its excellent organization and systematic presentation. Al-Kasani reorganized Hanafi law thematically rather than following the traditional chapter order, making it more accessible. The work provides detailed reasoning for each ruling and is considered one of the finest achievements of Hanafi legal literature.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The most studied Hanafi fiqh text in the Indian subcontinent and Central Asia. Al-Marghinani's commentary on his own primer Bidayat al-Mubtadi became the standard teaching text for intermediate Hanafi students and generated numerous super-commentaries. It balances brevity with sufficient detail for practical application.

subject: fiqh
description_en: An important Hanafi fiqh commentary on Kanz ad-Daqa'iq by an-Nasafi. Ibn Nujaym's sharp legal analysis and thorough discussion of evidence make this one of the most relied-upon references for Hanafi legal opinions. He passed away before completing it, and it was finished by at-Turi.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The definitive reference for Hanafi fatwa in the later period, known as the 'seal of the Hanafi school.' Ibn Abidin's super-commentary on ad-Durr al-Mukhtar resolves disputes within the school and incorporates the practical legal customs of Ottoman lands. It is the primary reference used by Hanafi muftis today.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A major Maliki fiqh commentary on the Mukhtasar of Khalil ibn Ishaq, which is the central text of later Maliki jurisprudence. Ad-Dardir's commentary is clear and well-organized, and together with ad-Dusuqi's super-commentary (Hashiyat ad-Dusuqi), it forms the primary reference for Maliki legal rulings.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The most authoritative super-commentary on ad-Dardir's Sharh al-Kabir and a primary reference for Maliki fatwa. Ad-Dusuqi clarifies ambiguities, corrects errors, and adds important legal distinctions. Together with ad-Dardir's commentary, this forms the standard reference pair for Maliki scholars in North and West Africa.

subject: fiqh
description_en: One of the two supreme references of later Shafi'i jurisprudence (alongside Tuhfat al-Muhtaj by Ibn Hajar al-Haytami). Ar-Ramli's commentary on an-Nawawi's Minhaj at-Talibin is comprehensive and authoritative, and where he and Ibn Hajar al-Haytami differ, Shafi'i scholars in Egypt and the Hijaz generally follow ar-Ramli.

subject: fiqh
description_en: One of the two supreme references of later Shafi'i jurisprudence. Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's commentary on an-Nawawi's Minhaj is exhaustive and deeply analytical. Where he and ar-Ramli differ, Shafi'i scholars in South Arabia, India, and Southeast Asia generally follow Ibn Hajar al-Haytami.

title_ar: الإنصاف في معرفة الراجح من الخلاف
title_en: Al-Insaf fi Ma'rifat ar-Rajih min al-Khilaf
subject: fiqh
description_en: The most important work for determining the relied-upon (mu'tamad) position within the Hanbali school. Al-Mardawi examines the various opinions within the school on thousands of issues, identifying which is the strongest position according to the school's methodology. It is indispensable for Hanbali scholars and muftis.

title_ar: المغني عن حمل الأسفار في الأسفار
subject: hadith_sciences
description_en: A comprehensive hadith verification (takhrij) of every hadith cited in al-Ghazali's Ihya Ulum ad-Din. Al-Iraqi traces each hadith to its source collections, grades its chain of narration, and identifies weak or fabricated narrations. This work is essential for anyone studying the Ihya, as it separates the authentic hadiths from the problematic ones.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A systematic collection of Shafi'i legal maxims (qawa'id fiqhiyyah) organized around the five universal maxims. As-Suyuti demonstrates how general principles govern thousands of specific rulings, making fiqh more coherent and accessible. It is widely studied alongside Ibn Nujaym's Hanafi counterpart.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The Hanafi counterpart to as-Suyuti's work on legal maxims. Ibn Nujaym systematizes Hanafi fiqh around general principles, showing how the five universal maxims and dozens of subsidiary rules organize the entire body of Hanafi law. The Majallat al-Ahkam al-Adliyyah (Ottoman Civil Code) drew heavily on this work.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A clear and accessible commentary on 99 legal maxims from the Majallat al-Ahkam al-Adliyyah (Ottoman Civil Code). Ahmad az-Zarqa explains each maxim with examples from Hanafi fiqh, making this an invaluable introduction to the science of legal maxims (qawa'id fiqhiyyah) for modern students.

subject: history
description_en: One of the most important works in the history of social science. Ibn Khaldun analyzes the rise and fall of civilizations through his concept of asabiyyah (group solidarity), developing what is essentially the first systematic philosophy of history. The work covers economics, education, politics, and urban development, anticipating many modern sociological theories.

subject: history
description_en: A monumental history from creation to the author's own time (14th century CE). Ibn Kathir combines Quranic narrative, hadith reports, and historical accounts to produce the most comprehensive Islamic history written in the medieval period. The early volumes on prophetic history and seerah are particularly valued.

title_en: Tarikh at-Tabari (History of Prophets and Kings)
subject: history
description_en: The most important general history in Islamic literature, covering events from creation to 915 CE. At-Tabari presents multiple accounts with full chains of transmission (isnad) for each event, allowing later scholars to evaluate the reliability of the reports. This methodology makes it a primary source for Islamic history.

subject: history
description_en: A comprehensive universal history from the beginning of time to 1231 CE, continuing where at-Tabari left off. Ibn al-Athir synthesizes multiple sources into a coherent narrative, making it more readable than at-Tabari's report-based approach. The sections on the Crusades and the Mongol invasion are especially valuable.

subject: comparative_religion
description_en: A pioneering work of comparative religion that catalogues and describes the major world religions and Islamic theological sects. Ash-Shahrastani describes each group's beliefs with relative objectivity, making this one of the first systematic works of religious studies. It covers Islamic sects, Judaism, Christianity, Zoroastrianism, Hinduism, and Greek philosophy.

subject: ethics
description_en: A collection of profound spiritual aphorisms that distill the essence of Islamic spirituality into concise, memorable statements. Ibn Ata'illah's hikam address the heart's relationship with Allah, trust in divine wisdom, and the spiritual meaning of life's circumstances. Numerous commentaries have been written on this work, which is studied across the Muslim world.

subject: biography
description_en: The most comprehensive biographical dictionary of Shafi'i scholars, covering over 1,500 entries from Imam ash-Shafi'i to as-Subki's own era. It is both a biographical reference and a defense of the Ash'ari-Shafi'i scholarly tradition, containing important theological discussions alongside the biographies.

subject: biography
description_en: One of the most important biographical dictionaries in Arabic literature, covering approximately 865 notable figures from all fields: scholars, poets, physicians, rulers, and more. Ibn Khallikan's elegant prose and attention to dates and personal details make it a primary source for medieval Islamic history.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: A creedal work written after Abu al-Hasan al-Ash'ari left the Mu'tazili school, in which he affirms the theology of Imam Ahmad and the early scholars. This work is significant for understanding al-Ash'ari's final theological positions and is cited by both Ash'ari theologians and those who advocate the Athari approach.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: An important early Ash'ari theological work that systematizes the arguments for God's existence, prophethood, and the Quran's inimitability (i'jaz). Al-Baqillani was one of the most formidable dialectical theologians in Islamic history, and this work represents a key stage in the development of Ash'ari kalam.

title_ar: الإرشاد إلى قواطع الأدلة في أصول الاعتقاد
subject: aqeedah
description_en: A mature Ash'ari theological treatise that represents the most developed form of kalam before al-Ghazali. Al-Juwayni addresses the attributes of God, prophethood, and eschatology with rigorous logical argumentation. His teacher-student lineage (al-Juwayni taught al-Ghazali) makes this work pivotal in Islamic intellectual history.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: The most detailed commentary on Imam at-Tahawi's famous creedal text. Ibn Abi al-Izz explains each statement of the Aqidah at-Tahawiyyah with extensive evidence from Quran, Sunnah, and the statements of the early scholars. This commentary leans toward the Athari/Salafi theological approach and is widely studied in Islamic universities.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: Al-Ghazali's systematic presentation of Ash'ari theology, written as a balanced middle path between excessive rationalism and pure traditionalism. The work covers God's existence, attributes, prophethood, and eschatology, representing al-Ghazali's mature theological thought after his spiritual crisis and recovery.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: A major theological and historical work written as a refutation of the Shia theologian Ibn al-Mutahhar al-Hilli. Ibn Taymiyyah defends Sunni positions on the Companions, the caliphate, and creedal issues, while also critiquing aspects of Ash'ari and Mu'tazili theology. It is one of the most important Sunni polemical works.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: The most comprehensive work of Maturidi theology, systematically presenting the theological positions of the Maturidi school with detailed rational arguments. Abu al-Mu'in an-Nasafi defends the Maturidi positions against Mu'tazili, Karramiyyah, and other theological opponents. It is the primary advanced reference for Maturidi kalam.

subject: aqeedah
description_en: The foundational text of the Maturidi school of theology, establishing the theological framework that would become dominant in the Hanafi world. Al-Maturidi addresses the existence of God, divine attributes, prophethood, and human agency with a distinctive methodology that balances reason and revelation differently from the Ash'ari approach.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: A modern, clear, and well-organized textbook on the principles of Islamic jurisprudence that has become standard at many Islamic universities. Az-Zuhayli presents usul al-fiqh in accessible academic language while maintaining scholarly rigor, covering sources of law, linguistic analysis, legal reasoning, and ijtihad.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: One of the four foundational works of usul al-fiqh in Sunni Islam. Al-Ghazali introduces Aristotelian logic as a prerequisite for legal reasoning and systematizes the principles of jurisprudence with philosophical precision. This work shaped the entire later tradition of usul al-fiqh, and its logical methodology became standard.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: A comprehensive and philosophically sophisticated work on usul al-fiqh that builds on al-Ghazali's Al-Mustasfa. Ar-Razi brings his formidable philosophical training to bear on legal theory, producing one of the most intellectually demanding works in the field. It influenced many later usul works including al-Amidi's Al-Ihkam.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: One of the four foundational works of usul al-fiqh. Al-Amidi synthesizes the methodologies of al-Ghazali and ar-Razi into a comprehensive and well-organized treatise that became the standard reference for later scholars. Many subsequent usul works are essentially commentaries or abridgments of al-Amidi.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: A concise and authoritative summary of usul al-fiqh that became the most studied text in the field within the Shafi'i tradition. As-Subki compresses the key positions of al-Amidi and others into a dense but comprehensive primer. Al-Mahalli's commentary and as-Suyuti's super-commentary on it are widely taught.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: The primary reference for Hanafi usul al-fiqh, presenting the distinctive Hanafi methodology of legal reasoning that differs from the Shafi'i-Ash'ari approach. As-Sarakhsi explains istihsan, urf (custom), and other Hanafi legal tools with thoroughness. It represents the Hanafi fuqaha' approach to usul as opposed to the mutakallimun approach.

subject: usul_fiqh
description_en: The most comprehensive commentary on Fakhr al-Islam al-Bazdawi's foundational Hanafi usul text. This commentary explains the dense original with extensive examples and comparative analysis. Together with Usul as-Sarakhsi, it forms the twin pillars of Hanafi legal methodology.

subject: fiqh
description_en: An important Hanbali fiqh manual by the grandfather of Taqi ad-Din ibn Taymiyyah. This work compiles the relied-upon positions of the Hanbali school with comparative notes on other opinions within the school. It was the basis for several important later commentaries and abridgments in Hanbali literature.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A comprehensive Hanbali fiqh work that systematically presents the various opinions within the school on every issue, making it invaluable for identifying the breadth of Hanbali jurisprudence. Ibn Muflih, a close student of Ibn Taymiyyah, includes his teacher's independent positions alongside the standard school positions.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The most studied Hanbali fiqh primer in Saudi Arabia and the Arabian Peninsula. Al-Hajjawi abridged Ibn Qudamah's Al-Muqni' into a concise text that covers the essential rulings of the Hanbali school. Ash-Sharh al-Mumti' by Ibn al-Uthaymeen is the most popular modern commentary on it.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A concise Hanbali fiqh primer that presents the relied-upon positions of the later Hanbali school in clear, accessible language. It is widely studied as a beginner's text in Hanbali fiqh and has generated several important commentaries, notably Manar as-Sabil by Ibrahim ibn Duwayyan.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A well-organized and evidence-based commentary on Dalil at-Talib that became widely used in Hanbali studies. Ibn Duwayyan provides Quranic and hadith evidence for each ruling, making this a practical reference for deriving rulings from primary sources. Al-Albani's Irwa al-Ghalil provides hadith verification for this work.

title_ar: المقنع في فقه إمام السنة أحمد بن حنبل
subject: fiqh
description_en: A concise yet comprehensive Hanbali fiqh manual by the author of Al-Mughni. While Al-Mughni provides extensive comparative discussion, Al-Muqni' focuses on the Hanbali school's positions. It became the basis for many later commentaries and abridgments, including the widely studied Zad al-Mustaqni'.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A beginner's text in Shafi'i fiqh that is widely studied in Southeast Asia, East Africa, and the Hadrami diaspora. Ba-Fadl presents the essential rulings in a concise format suitable for new students. The most famous commentary is Bushra al-Karim by Sa'id Ba-Ishn.

title_en: Fath al-Mu'in bi Sharh Qurrat al-Ayn
subject: fiqh
description_en: An important Shafi'i fiqh text widely studied in South and Southeast Asia. Al-Malibari, an Indian scholar, provides a detailed commentary that became the basis for the famous super-commentary I'anat at-Talibin. It focuses on practical rulings and is valued for its clarity.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A widely used super-commentary on Fath al-Mu'in that is one of the most popular Shafi'i fiqh references in Southeast Asia, particularly in Indonesia, Malaysia, and Thailand. Ad-Dimyati adds extensive notes, elaborations, and practical guidance that make the work accessible to students and scholars alike.

title_ar: مغني المحتاج إلى معرفة معاني ألفاظ المنهاج
subject: fiqh
description_en: An important Shafi'i fiqh commentary on an-Nawawi's Minhaj at-Talibin that, alongside Tuhfat al-Muhtaj and Nihayat al-Muhtaj, forms the trio of authoritative later Shafi'i references. Al-Khatib ash-Shirbini's work is particularly valued for its clear exposition and for providing accessible explanations of difficult passages.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The most important foundational text of the Maliki school after the Muwatta itself. Sahnun compiled the legal opinions transmitted from Imam Malik through his student Ibn al-Qasim into a comprehensive fiqh work covering all chapters of law. It is the primary source for early Maliki jurisprudence.

subject: fiqh
description_en: An influential abridgment of the Mudawwanah that reorganized and streamlined the material for easier study. Al-Baradhi'i's work became the basis for later teaching in the Maliki school, particularly in North and West Africa, and influenced the composition of subsequent Maliki manuals.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The most famous beginner's text in Maliki fiqh, studied across North and West Africa for over a thousand years. Ibn Abi Zayd, known as 'the little Malik' for his encyclopedic knowledge of the school, compressed the essential rulings into a concise primer. The opening chapter on creed is itself a widely studied text.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A comprehensive Maliki fiqh manual by the great Andalusian hadith scholar Ibn Abd al-Barr. Unlike his other works which are comparative, this book focuses specifically on the Maliki school's positions as practiced in Medina. It is valued for combining legal rulings with their hadith evidence.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A unique comparative fiqh manual that presents the positions of all four Sunni schools on each issue in a clear, organized format. Ibn Juzayy, a Maliki scholar from Granada, created an accessible reference that allows readers to compare the schools' positions side by side. It remains popular for its clarity and comprehensiveness.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A major Maliki fiqh encyclopedia by one of the greatest legal minds of Islamic history. Al-Qarafi, known for his expertise in legal maxims and the distinction between fatwa and qada (judicial ruling), produces a comprehensive work that goes beyond mere Maliki positions to analyze the reasoning behind the law.

subject: fiqh
description_en: A groundbreaking work that identifies 548 distinctions (furuq) between apparently similar legal concepts. Al-Qarafi shows that superficially similar cases may have different rulings due to subtle legal differences. This work revolutionized legal thinking and is essential for understanding the precision of Islamic jurisprudence.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The most comprehensive work of Zahiri (literalist) jurisprudence, covering the entire range of Islamic law from a strict textualist perspective. Ibn Hazm rejects analogy (qiyas) and derives all rulings directly from Quran, Sunnah, and consensus. Even non-Zahiri scholars consult this work for its rigorous hadith analysis and unique legal perspectives.

subject: fiqh
description_en: The foundational fiqh work of Imam ash-Shafi'i containing his 'new' (jadid) legal opinions formulated in Egypt. It covers the full range of Islamic law with detailed evidence from Quran, Sunnah, and ijma. This work, along with Ar-Risalah on methodology, established the Shafi'i school and transformed Islamic legal thinking.

subject: hadith
description_en: A comprehensive hadith commentary that collects narrations organized by fiqh topics with explanations of their meanings and legal implications. Al-Baghawi, known as 'the Reviver of the Sunnah,' provides clear and accessible explanations drawing on the positions of all four schools. It also classifies hadiths by their strength.

subject: hadith
description_en: A well-organized hadith collection that classifies narrations into 'sahih' (from Bukhari and Muslim) and 'hasan' (from the other collections). Al-Baghawi provides a practical arrangement that made it widely used for teaching. Al-Khatib at-Tibrizi later expanded it into the famous Mishkat al-Masabih.

subject: hadith
description_en: An expansion of al-Baghawi's Masabih as-Sunnah that adds a third category of hadiths from additional sources. At-Tibrizi provides full isnad references and additional narrations, making it one of the most comprehensive topically organized hadith collections.

subject: hadith
description_en: A hadith collection focused on manners, etiquette, and social conduct in Islam. Al-Bukhari compiled narrations covering kindness to parents, neighbors' rights, greeting etiquette, humor, and daily social interactions.

title_ar: تبيين كذب المفتري فيما نسب إلى الإمام أبي الحسن الأشعري
subject: aqeedah
description_en: A biographical and theological defense of Abu al-Hasan al-Ash'ari and the Ash'ari school by the great Damascene historian.

subject: history
description_en: The largest biographical dictionary in Arabic literature, covering everyone of note who lived in or visited Damascus.

subject: biography
description_en: A masterful biographical encyclopedia covering the most notable figures in Islamic history from the Prophet's time to adh-Dhahabi's era.

subject: biography
description_en: The most comprehensive biographical dictionary of the Companions of the Prophet, covering over 12,000 entries.

subject: biography
description_en: A major biographical dictionary of the Companions that synthesizes four earlier works.

subject: biography
description_en: A biographical dictionary of pious Muslims emphasizing their spiritual states, devotional practices, and wise sayings.

subject: ethics
description_en: An abridgment of Ibn al-Jawzi's Minhaj al-Qasidin, a Hanbali-friendly version of al-Ghazali's Ihya.

subject: ethics
description_en: A beautiful treatise on dhikr and its 70+ benefits for the heart and soul.
